Title: Data Scientist Engineer
Description:
Devoteam is a leading consulting firm focused on digital transformation. We help our clients leverage technology to achieve their business goals.

Our longstanding partnerships with leaders like AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft, and ServiceNow amplify our capabilities, allowing us to deliver top-tier expertise and award-winning services. We complement these partnerships with in-depth Cyber, Data & AI, Software development and multi cloud expertise.

For 30 years, we have always been guiding our customers on the next tech wave. Devoteam has guided organizations through the Internet and cloud. The next revolution? AI.

With 11,000 tech natives working in 25+ countries, we bring you highly skilled IT experts across Europe

At Devoteam, tech is in our DNA, and it’s the people we empower with tech. Now, with responsible AI, we’re ready to unlock a future where technology drives positive change.

Job Description

Are you passionate about data-driven platforms, machine learning models, and innovative applications? As a Data Scientist/Engineer at Devoteam, you'll play a hands-on role in delivering impactful solutions across various clients and industries. Each mission we undertake has clear deliverables and deadlines, ensuring you gain diverse experience. For example, one month you might develop a predictive maintenance model, and the next, a customer segmentation model for a retail client.

Key Responsibilities:


Platform Setup: Design and implement robust data platforms using Azure, AWS or Google Cloud and Databricks.
Data Management: Gather, prepare, and manage data for analysis.
Model Research: Investigate and select appropriate machine learning models.
ML Modeling: Develop and refine machine learning models.
Client Collaboration: Spend approximately 50% of your time working directly with clients, either independently or as part of a team.



Your Role:


Build and maintain data platforms and pipelines using Azure, AWS or Google Cloud, Databricks, and Python.
Develop predictive and machine learning models tailored to client needs.
Advise clients on translating their business requirements into technical solutions using machine learning techniques.
Stay updated on the latest trends in machine learning and AI, sharing insights with clients.
Act as a strategic partner to client Data Science teams, helping them navigate their challenges and aspirations.
Inspire clients with new data-driven opportunities.
Bring best practices in Git, DevOps, and CICD to enhance client data platforms.
Collaborate with Data Scientists, Data Engineers, and Business Stakeholders.
Represent Devoteam both online and offline, showcasing our expertise.



Qualifications

About You:


Reside in Belgium. (Mandatory)
Hold a university degree in Computer Science, (Business) Engineering, or a specialized master's in Machine Learning or AI.
Minimum 3 years experience in a similar role.
Have a solid understanding of various machine learning models (e.g., SVM, RNN, Deep Learning) and their applications.
Possess foundational knowledge of Data Science, including sampling techniques and evaluation metrics.
Proficient in SQL and Python.
Experience with Spark and/or cloud ecosystems is a must.
Experience with Databricks is real asset
Eager to learn and passionate about data and machine learning technologies.
Excited to join a dynamic and growing team.
Fluent in English; proficiency in Dutch or French is mandatory
Sponsorship for a work permit not possible



Additional Information

Why choose us?


Thrive in a fast-growing, entrepreneurial environment
Work with innovative and impactful Cloud, Data & AI projects for industry-leading clients
Connect with extraordinary talents in a collaborative and diverse culture
Enjoy a healthy work-life balance in an inspiring, eco-friendly workplace
Competitive compensation package



At Devoteam, we combine strong values – respect, frankness, ambition, entrepreneurship & collaboration – with a fun and supportive environment that empowers you to innovate, grow, and succeed in the fast-paced world of Cloud, Data & AI.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
We are seeking a driven and creative Data Scientist with a minimum of 3 years of experience in a data science or machine learning role, who will be instrumental in building predictive models, testing hypotheses, and providing statistically sound insights that enhance our lead generation capabilities. The Data Scientist will be part of our analytics team, working closely with IT, marketing, media buying and CRO teams to transform raw data into actionable insights, whether that means building real-time scoring models, investigating campaign performance, or helping the business distinguish signal from noise when making decisions.

The ideal candidate is someone who thrives on solving ambiguous problems, brings strong statistical intuition alongside machine learning skills, and can serve as a trusted analytical partner to stakeholders across the business. They're as comfortable building production ML pipelines as they are answering a media buyer's question about whether a source is actually underperforming or just experiencing variance. Experience in digital marketing, lead generation, or ad-tech is highly desirable.

Primary Responsibilities


Leverage agentic AI-assisted development tools throughout the workflow, from exploratory analysis and feature engineering to model development and pipeline building, to accelerate iteration speed while maintaining rigor and code quality
Design, develop, and deploy machine learning models to segment and score landing page visitors based on intent and lead quality, using behavioral signals such as scroll depth, click patterns, session duration, and form engagement.
Engineer predictive features from raw user behavior data to improve model accuracy and business relevance.
Build and maintain real-time scoring pipelines that enable dynamic postback signals to ad networks, improving campaign optimization speed and efficiency.
Collaborate with marketing and analytics teams to define quality/intent tiers and translate business logic into quantifiable model outputs.
Conduct exploratory data analysis and experimentation to uncover patterns in user behavior that correlate with downstream lead quality and conversion outcomes.
Continuously monitor, evaluate, and iterate on model performance, ensuring alignment with evolving business goals and traffic patterns.
Contribute to forecasting and budget projection initiatives as needed, supporting strategic planning with data-driven modeling.
Clearly communicate, document and present findings, methodologies, and model specifications for cross-functional, non-technical stakeholders.
Support data-driven decision-making by quantifying uncertainty, assessing statistical significance, and challenging assumptions when the data doesn't support them.
Identify and quantify relationships between behavioral signals, traffic sources, campaign attributes, and lead quality outcomes; distinguish meaningful correlations from spurious ones.
Serve as an analytical partner to media buyers and marketing teams: investigate campaign and source performance, validate hypotheses about lead quality, and provide statistically rigorous answers to business questions.


Requirements:


3+ years of experience in data science, machine learning, or a related quantitative role.
Comfortable working in agentic AI-assisted development environments (e.g., Cursor, ClaudeCode, Antigravity) to accelerate model development, data exploration, and pipeline building, including the judgment to know when AI-generated outputs need manual verification or refinement.
Strong proficiency in Python, including ML libraries such as scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, or similar.
Solid experience with feature engineering, particularly from behavioral, clickstream, or event-based data.
Familiarity with deploying models in production environments; experience with real-time or near-real-time inference is a strong plus.
Strong foundation in statistical inference, hypothesis testing and root-cause analysis: can distinguish signal from noise, assess significance, and communicate uncertainty clearly.
Proficiency in SQL and experience working with large-scale datasets.
Understanding of classification, regression, and clustering techniques; experience with user segmentation or propensity modeling preferred.
Exposure to digital marketing concepts and KPIs (CPL, CVR, ROAS, etc.) is highly desirable.
Ability to translate vague business questions into testable hypotheses and structured analyses.
Excellent problem-solving skills with the ability to work through ambiguity and define structure where none exists.
Strong communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Team-oriented mindset with a collaborative and proactive approach.
Upper-Intermediate or higher level of English.


Nice to Have


Experience building or using custom skills, prompt workflows, or tool-use patterns within agentic coding environments.
Experience in lead generation, ad-tech, or performance marketing environments.
Familiarity with ad network optimization, postback mechanisms, or conversion APIs.
Experience with cloud platforms and MLOps tools.
Background in time-series forecasting or demand modeling.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
HTML:
Wat mag je verwachten


Bij e5 Fashion draait alles rond onze klanten: begrijpen wat ze zoeken, hoe ze winkelen en hoe we hen nog beter kunnen inspireren en bedienen

.Als Machine Learning Engineer help jij ons om klantgedrag, verkoop, voorraad en assortiment slimmer te begrijpen. Je ontdekt patronen en verbanden in onze data die vandaag nog niet altijd zichtbaar zijn, en vertaalt die naar inzichten waarmee we betere beslissingen nemen voor onze klanten, boetieks en webshop. Zo bouw je mee aan een datagedreven e5, waar technologie en AI bijdragen aan een sterkere klantbeleving.


Een sleutelrol in een ambitieus data- en AI-traject binnen e5. Je analyseert data en ontdekt patronen, correlaties en inzichten die vandaag nog niet altijd zichtbaar zijn. Je onderzoekt welke factoren onze verkoop, voorraad, assortiment, klantbeleving en businessprocessen beïnvloeden, en vertaalt die inzichten naar concrete aanbevelingen. Je bouwt en verbetert Machine Learning-modellen rond thema’s zoals forecasting, segmentatie, aanbevelingen en optimalisatie. Daarbij denk je niet alleen technisch, maar ook businessgericht. Welke inzichten helpen ons om beter te sturen, slimmer te plannen of sneller bij te sturen? Je werkt nauw samen met collega’s uit verschillende teams en met een externe partner, zodat je kan sparren, groeien en mee bouwen aan ons data- en AI-platform. Dit is geen standaard ML-job. Je krijgt de kans om mee vorm te geven aan hoe e5 data en AI inzet in de praktijk, dicht bij de business met zichtbare impact op klanten, boetieks, webshop en resultaten. Je komt terecht in een omgeving met een sterk teamgevoel, hands-on mentaliteit, korte lijnen en collega’s met goesting om vooruit te gaan. We bieden je een marktconform loon met extralegale voordelen en personeelskorting op onze collectie, ook voor familie en vrienden.


Great style starts with great people


Bij e5 Fashion draait alles rond mensen - in wat ze dragen én in waar ze werk

en. Je komt terecht bij een fashionretailer in volle beweging, waar boetieks, webshop en ondersteunende teams elke dag samenwerken aan een sterkere klantbeleving. We houden van korte lijnen, duidelijke afspraken en oplossingen die werken in de praktijk .Je krijgt vertrouwen en verantwoordelijkheid, maar ook ruimte om mee richting te geven. We schakelen snel, denken vooruit en werken aan projecten die zichtbaar verschil maken voor onze klanten, collega’s en resultaten.


Waarom pas jij hier


  • Je hebt een master in een kwantitatieve richting, zoals Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Wiskunde, Econometrie of gelijkwaardig door ervaring;
  • Je hebt ervaring met Machine Learning, bijvoorbeeld forecasting, clustering, segmentatie of aanbevelingsmodellen;
  • Je werkt vlot met Python en SQL;
  • Je kan data kritisch analyseren en verbanden leggen die niet meteen zichtbaar zijn;
  • Je denkt mee over welke data, datapunten en KPI’s ons helpen om betere beslissingen te nemen voor klanten, boetieks en webshop;
  • Je bent nieuwsgierig, analytisch en durft hypotheses challengen;
  • Je vertaalt inzichten helder naar concrete verbeteringen of beslissingen;
  • Je werkt graag op het snijpunt van data, business en technologie;
  • Ervaring met cloud- of dataplatformen zoals BigQuery of GCP is een plus;
  • Kennis van data pipelines, MLOps of model deployment is een plus;
  • Affiniteit met retail, e-commerce, klantgedrag of voorraadbeheer is mooi meegenomen;
  • Je spreekt vlot Nederlands en Engels.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
About SailPeak

At Sailpeak, we are a Brussels-based consulting firm specializing in guiding Banking & Insurance firms through the complexities of digital transformation.

With expertise in Business Strategy, Data & AI, and Digital Transformation, Sailpeak helps clients navigate the ever-evolving landscape and reach new heights of success.

🤖 About The Role

We are looking for a Data Scientist with 1-3 years of experience, working on AI-related projects to join our growing team.

In this role, you will drive digital strategy for top-tier banking clients by delivering high-impact projects focused on data analysis, performance optimization, and dashboard creation. You will play a pivotal part in expanding our internal AI capabilities, gaining hands-on experience with cutting-edge machine learning models to build innovative solutions for the financial services sector.

📊 You will:


Assist in data collection, cleansing, and transformation from diverse banking and fintech data sources, ensuring high-quality inputs for advanced analytics.
Contribute to Machine Learning and Deep Learning projects, gaining exposure to AI models, intelligent agents, and digital assistants applied within the financial services sector.
Support the development of data-driven digital strategies and AI-powered campaigns tailored to banking clients.
Conduct A/B testing, hypothesis validation, and performance analysis of digital and AI-driven initiatives in the banking domain.
Create and maintain interactive dashboards, visualizations, and reports to monitor key metrics for digital banking strategies and AI adoption.
Collaborate with cross-functional and agile teams to optimize campaign segmentation, targeting, and personalization, leveraging predictive analytics.
Participate in data interpretation and storytelling, contributing to actionable recommendations that support banking digital transformation and AI initiatives.


📊 You bring to the table:


1-3 years of experience as a Data Scientist
A Master’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Banking, Digital Transformation, or a related field.
A strong eagerness to work with data, AI, and machine learning models, and to grow expertise in digital transformation within banking.
Basic knowledge of data analysis and visualization tools such as Python, SQL, Power BI, Tableau, or VBA.
Familiarity with digital banking platforms, fintech ecosystems, and emerging AI technologies is a plus.
An interest in AI, intelligent agents, and digital assistants, and how they reshape customer journeys and operational efficiency in financial services.
Strong communication and data storytelling skills to translate insights into value for digital strategy and AI adoption.


🗣️ Language Skills

Fluency in English and French/Dutch

🤹 Other Skills


Experience with Scrum or Agile methods is a great plus for us.
We are looking for proactive problem-solvers
You’ll be working independently & autonomously
Passion for growing, building, and continuous learning, especially around the developments within AI and Data Science


🎁 What’s In It For You

💸 A competitive salary and benefits package.

🦾 Working on cutting-edge projects with leading banking clients, in a startup environment

💿 Exposure to a wide array of AI and data science technologies.

🫂 Mentorship from Directors and Partners with backgrounds from renowned companies like Google, PayPal, Semetis, and Sia Partners.

🧑‍🔬 The Process


Step 1: 30-minute call with our Head of Talent (Peter): focusing on your current situation, motivation, experience, and practicalities
Step 2: 45-minute deep dive into the projects you’ve worked on with one of our experienced consultants
Step 3: 30-minute call with one of the founders, talking in more depth about motivations and ambitions
Step 4: Onsite Business Case in our office:)
The Offer


Are you ready to embark on a rewarding career in management consulting with Sailpeak?

Title: Data Scientist - McKinsey Transformation
Description:
Who You'll Work With

Driving lasting impact and building long-term capabilities with our clients is not easy work. You are the kind of person who thrives in a high performance/high reward culture - doing hard things, picking yourself up when you stumble, and having the resilience to try another way forward.

In return for your drive, determination, and curiosity, we'll provide the resources, mentorship, and opportunities you need to become a stronger leader faster than you ever thought possible. Your colleagues—at all levels—will invest deeply in your development, just as much as they invest in delivering exceptional results for clients. Every day, you'll receive apprenticeship, coaching, and exposure that will accelerate your growth in ways you won’t find anywhere else.

When you join us, you will have:


Continuous learning: Our learning and apprenticeship culture, backed by structured programs, is all about helping you grow while creating an environment where feedback is clear, actionable, and focused on your development. The real magic happens when you take the input from others to heart and embrace the fast-paced learning experience, owning your journey.
A voice that matters: From day one, we value your ideas and contributions. You’ll make a tangible impact by offering innovative ideas and practical solutions, all while upholding our unwavering commitment to ethics and integrity. We not only encourage diverse perspectives, but they are critical in driving us toward the best possible outcomes.
Global community: With colleagues across 65+ countries and over 100 different nationalities, our firm’s diversity fuels creativity and helps us come up with the best solutions for our clients. Plus, you’ll have the opportunity to learn from exceptional colleagues with diverse backgrounds and experiences.
World-class benefits: On top of a competitive salary (based on your location, experience, and skills), we provide a comprehensive benefits package to enable holistic well-being for you and your family.


Your Impact

Working closely with a cross-functional team of data scientists, data engineers, product developers, and analytics-focused consultants, your core responsibility involves collaborating with product management to build performant, robust, and maintainable analytics products that adhere to established data-science approaches and best practices.

You will expand your expertise across various analytics topics, encompassing descriptive analytics as well as the design, development, and refinement of statistical models, optimization techniques, advanced machine learning, and predictive models like boosted trees.

Furthermore, driving the fine-tuning and evaluation of LLMs requires leveraging the latest advancements in neural network architectures and machine learning techniques, optimizing their performance and efficiency particularly for segmentation use cases. Accelerating this development and problem-solving process involves utilizing cutting-edge GenAI tools such as Claude and Cursor, alongside implementing efficient development and maintenance workflows (MLOps) within our Databricks platform.

Translating these complex data analyses into actionable analytical insights directly guides client project directions, while simultaneously fueling your contributions to internal knowledge sharing, research, and technical documentation focused on unstructured data, LLMs, and predictive modeling. Ultimately, all these efforts converge to bring advanced analytics capabilities into one of the firm's flagship products, "Wave" serving as the backbone for how McKinsey runs future transformations and utilizing powerful data science assets to dramatically improve the odds of success for our clients.

You will work in our McKinsey Client Capabilities Network in EMEA and will be part of our Wave Transformatics team.

Wave is a McKinsey SaaS product that equips clients to successfully manage improvement programs and transformations. Focused on business impact, Wave allows clients to track the impact of individual initiatives and understand how they affect longer term goals. It is a mix of an intuitive interface and McKinsey business expertise that gives clients a simple and insightful picture of what can otherwise be a complex process by allowing them to track the progress and performance of initiatives against business goals, budget and time frames.

Our Transformatics team builds data and AI products to provide analytics insights to clients and McKinsey teams involved in transformation programs across the globe. The current team is composed of 3 data engineers, 5 data scientists,1 analyst and 2 PMs who are spread across several geographies. The team covers a variety of industries, functions, analytics methodologies and platforms – e.g. Cloud data engineering, advanced statistics, machine learning, predictive analytics, MLOps and generative AI.

Your Qualifications and Skills


MSc or PhD level in the field of computer science, machine learning, applied statistics, mathematics or equivalent by experience
1+ years of experience in data science, statistical modelling (e.g. advanced regressions, clustering, classification models) and machine learning techniques (e.g. random forest, support vector machines, gradient boosting, XGBoost)
Programming experience in the following languages: Python and SQL. Basic understanding of PySpark
Comfortable leveraging the latest GenAI tools (such as Cursor or Claude) to accelerate coding, debugging, and problem-solving.
Experience with version control using Git and collaborative development workflows in GitHub (e.g., branching, pull requests, code reviews, and repository management)
Experience collaborating on projects to deploy advanced analytics and data science methods in real-world organizations (CI/CD, deployment observation, etc...)
Familiarity with LLMs agents, RAG systems, prompt engineering, machine learning libraries (scikit-learn) or deep learning libraries (TensorFlow/PyTorch) is a plus
Exposure to tools like Databricks and Tableau is a plus
Effective communication skills, including a readiness to break down analytical concepts, methods, and results for non-technical stakeholders. (e.g. consultants, etc...)
Show more
HTML:
Who You'll Work With

Driving lasting impact and building long-term capabilities with our clients is not easy work. You are the kind of person who thrives in a high performance/high reward culture - doing hard things, picking yourself up when you stumble, and having the resilience to try another way forward.

In return for your drive, determination, and curiosity, we'll provide the resources, mentorship, and opportunities you need to become a stronger leader faster than you ever thought possible. Your colleagues—at all levels—will invest deeply in your development, just as much as they invest in delivering exceptional results for clients. Every day, you'll receive apprenticeship, coaching, and exposure that will accelerate your growth in ways you won’t find anywhere else.

When you join us, you will have:

  • Continuous learning: Our learning and apprenticeship culture, backed by structured programs, is all about helping you grow while creating an environment where feedback is clear, actionable, and focused on your development. The real magic happens when you take the input from others to heart and embrace the fast-paced learning experience, owning your journey.
  • A voice that matters: From day one, we value your ideas and contributions. You’ll make a tangible impact by offering innovative ideas and practical solutions, all while upholding our unwavering commitment to ethics and integrity. We not only encourage diverse perspectives, but they are critical in driving us toward the best possible outcomes.
  • Global community: With colleagues across 65+ countries and over 100 different nationalities, our firm’s diversity fuels creativity and helps us come up with the best solutions for our clients. Plus, you’ll have the opportunity to learn from exceptional colleagues with diverse backgrounds and experiences.
  • World-class benefits: On top of a competitive salary (based on your location, experience, and skills), we provide a comprehensive benefits package to enable holistic well-being for you and your family.

Your Impact

Working closely with a cross-functional team of data scientists, data engineers, product developers, and analytics-focused consultants, your core responsibility involves collaborating with product management to build performant, robust, and maintainable analytics products that adhere to established data-science approaches and best practices.

You will expand your expertise across various analytics topics, encompassing descriptive analytics as well as the design, development, and refinement of statistical models, optimization techniques, advanced machine learning, and predictive models like boosted trees.

Furthermore, driving the fine-tuning and evaluation of LLMs requires leveraging the latest advancements in neural network architectures and machine learning techniques, optimizing their performance and efficiency particularly for segmentation use cases. Accelerating this development and problem-solving process involves utilizing cutting-edge GenAI tools such as Claude and Cursor, alongside implementing efficient development and maintenance workflows (MLOps) within our Databricks platform.

Translating these complex data analyses into actionable analytical insights directly guides client project directions, while simultaneously fueling your contributions to internal knowledge sharing, research, and technical documentation focused on unstructured data, LLMs, and predictive modeling. Ultimately, all these efforts converge to bring advanced analytics capabilities into one of the firm's flagship products, "Wave" serving as the backbone for how McKinsey runs future transformations and utilizing powerful data science assets to dramatically improve the odds of success for our clients.

You will work in our McKinsey Client Capabilities Network in EMEA and will be part of our Wave Transformatics team.

Wave is a McKinsey SaaS product that equips clients to successfully manage improvement programs and transformations. Focused on business impact, Wave allows clients to track the impact of individual initiatives and understand how they affect longer term goals. It is a mix of an intuitive interface and McKinsey business expertise that gives clients a simple and insightful picture of what can otherwise be a complex process by allowing them to track the progress and performance of initiatives against business goals, budget and time frames.

Our Transformatics team builds data and AI products to provide analytics insights to clients and McKinsey teams involved in transformation programs across the globe. The current team is composed of 3 data engineers, 5 data scientists,1 analyst and 2 PMs who are spread across several geographies. The team covers a variety of industries, functions, analytics methodologies and platforms – e.g. Cloud data engineering, advanced statistics, machine learning, predictive analytics, MLOps and generative AI.

Your Qualifications and Skills

  • MSc or PhD level in the field of computer science, machine learning, applied statistics, mathematics or equivalent by experience
  • 1+ years of experience in data science, statistical modelling (e.g. advanced regressions, clustering, classification models) and machine learning techniques (e.g. random forest, support vector machines, gradient boosting, XGBoost)
  • Programming experience in the following languages: Python and SQL. Basic understanding of PySpark
  • Comfortable leveraging the latest GenAI tools (such as Cursor or Claude) to accelerate coding, debugging, and problem-solving.
  • Experience with version control using Git and collaborative development workflows in GitHub (e.g., branching, pull requests, code reviews, and repository management)
  • Experience collaborating on projects to deploy advanced analytics and data science methods in real-world organizations (CI/CD, deployment observation, etc...)
  • Familiarity with LLMs agents, RAG systems, prompt engineering, machine learning libraries (scikit-learn) or deep learning libraries (TensorFlow/PyTorch) is a plus
  • Exposure to tools like Databricks and Tableau is a plus
  • Effective communication skills, including a readiness to break down analytical concepts, methods, and results for non-technical stakeholders. (e.g. consultants, etc...)

Title: Financial Risk Modeler - Data Scientist
Description:
Can’t wait to make an impact on the world? You’re not alone. Join us in driving progress in the working world and beyond.

Your journey with us

Deloitte Belgium’s Financial Risk department helps organizations identify the risks impacting their business and implement effective and efficient processes to measure and manage these risks (mainly market, credit and liquidity risk). To this end, the team leverages its expertise in data analytics, machine learning techniques and process optimization, combined with a strong understanding of the regulatory and business context.

As a Quantitative Modeler, you will be developing, validating and implementing advanced mathematical and statistical models to address complex financial and business challenges. The ideal candidate will have a strong academic background, experience in quantitative methods, and a deep understanding of financial instruments and markets.

What will you be doing?

You Will Join a Team Of Experienced Risk Modelers Where Collaboration And Communication Are At The Heart Of Our Way Of Working. You Are Curious And Analytical, With a Natural Drive To Excel. Amongst Other, Your Activities Will Include


Model Development: Design and develop quantitative models for risk management, utilizing techniques such as Monte Carlo simulation and machine learning.
Model Validation: Conduct rigorous back-testing and validation of models, ensuring their robustness and alignment with regulation and market conditions.
Data Analysis & Interpretation: Analyze large datasets to identify trends, relationships, and patterns. Work with financial and market data to derive insights for model improvement and validation.
Process Optimization: Analyze process workflow and advise on improvements to facilitate experience in model application, maintenance, and internal and regulatory reporting.
Research & Innovation: Stay up-to-date with the latest research in quantitative finance, machine learning, and statistical techniques. Apply new methodologies and tools to improve existing models and develop new strategies.


Let's Talk About You


You have a Master's degree in a quantitative field such as Mathematics, Physics, Statistics, Business or Financial Engineering / Economics or Computer Science.
You have between 2 and 5 years of experience in quantitative modeling and/or validation, preferably in financial services, with hands-on experience with programming languages such as Python, R, Matlab or SAS/SQL.
You demonstrate understanding of business processes, regulatory requirements, internal control risk management, process, data and technology controls and related standards and frameworks.
You are fluent in English and Dutch or French.


Soft Skills


Quantitative and qualitative problem-solving skills, with high capacity for solving challenging business problems
Present complex quantitative concepts and findings in a clear and accessible manner to stakeholders at various levels of technical expertise.
Strong analytical thinking and a drive for continuous learning and improvement, tech-savvy
Ability to work in a collaborative, team-based environment


How will you grow?


You will further develop your expertise across a broad range of quantitative applications in risk.
As you grow, you will be given more responsibility to manage assignments working directly with senior members of our client teams and guide junior colleagues.
As you become proficient in various skill sets, you will coach your colleagues and continue to enhance your management competencies.


Our story

Our Risk, Regulatory & Forensic practice is a global leader in helping clients manage risk and uncertainty from the boardroom to the network. We provide a broad array of services that allow our clients around the world to better measure, manage and control risk to enhance the reliability of systems and processes throughout their organization.

Who is Deloitte?

We provide industry-leading audit and assurance, tax and legal, consulting and related services. We are committed to driving innovation across offerings to help our clients address their challenges, while giving our professionals opportunities to learn and grow in this era of transformation.

In Belgium, +5000 dedicated professionals active in +10 offices, take great pride in bringing multidisciplinary expertise to a wide variety of clients, from national and international companies, small, fast-growing and large organizations to public institutions and governmental authorities.

Why Deloitte?

Be the true you! We foster diversity and inclusion and encourage you to bring your authentic self to work. Explore, question and collaborate while building a career that inspires and energises you.

Never stop growing! Diversity of thought makes us stronger. At Deloitte, we tailor a personalized learning experience, offering you the opportunity to grow at your own pace and achieve maximum impact.

We practice what we preach! As a Purpose-led organisation, at the heart of everything we do is a set of timeless principles and unifying values .

Life looks different for each of us, so we created a varied benefits package that you can tap into:


My Benefits My Choice, a flexible rewards plan tailored to your lifestyle and priorities
Sustainable transport options offered by Mobility@Deloitte
Flexible work arrangements for all and initiatives supported by Parents & Caregivers @Deloitte
Wellbeing tips and activities powered by Energise@Deloitte
Topped off with other health benefits and insurance opportunities


Empowering our employees with flexible work arrangements remains essential in today's reality:


Hybrid workplace: combination of home office and on-site (+10 offices in Belgium or client's premises).
Part-time employment: all our jobs are open to full-time or part-time work under a 90% or 80% regime.


Join us to make an impact together! Apply now!

Title: Data Scientist – Machine Learning & AI Job ID: JP054484
Description:
About the job Data Scientist – Machine Learning & AI Job ID: JP054484

Data Scientist – Machine Learning & AI

We are seeking a Data Scientist to design, develop, and maintain a modular and future-proof machine learning framework while delivering advanced analytics and predictive modeling solutions to support business objectives.

Key Responsibilities Machine Learning Framework Development


Design and implement a scalable and reusable machine learning framework.
Establish best practices for data preprocessing, model training, validation, deployment, and lifecycle management.
Implement monitoring, feedback loops, and model retraining mechanisms.
Manage model versioning, reproducibility, logging, and system monitoring.
Prepare infrastructure for future cloud migration initiatives.
Document processes, methodologies, and technical solutions.
Coach and support team members in the adoption and use of the framework.
Present technical analyses and results to business stakeholders.


Data Science & Machine Learning Solutions


Translate business requirements into technical solutions using an iterative development approach.
Identify, assess, and validate relevant data sources.
Perform data analysis, data preparation, and exploratory data analysis (EDA).
Develop feature engineering strategies to improve model performance.
Select, evaluate, compare, and optimize machine learning algorithms.
Build, tune, automate, and maintain predictive models.
Continuously evaluate and improve model accuracy and effectiveness.
Communicate insights and recommendations through clear visualizations and presentations.


Required Skills & Experience


Strong experience with Python for Data Science and Machine Learning.
Solid knowledge of SQL and database technologies.
Experience with Machine Learning and AI techniques for data analysis.
Experience developing and evaluating analytical and predictive models.
Hands-on experience with Jupyter Notebook and KNIME.
Knowledge of Microsoft Fabric.
Experience with Microsoft DevOps or similar CI/CD and version control platforms.
Experience working with Microsoft SQL databases.
Knowledge of Oracle database environments.
Familiarity with Azure Machine Learning and Azure ML SDK.


Languages


Native proficiency in either Dutch or French.
Ability to read and understand the other national language.


Working Model


Hybrid work environment.
On-site presence required 2 days per week in Brussels, with remote work available on remaining days.


Selection Process


Interview combined with a practical technical assignment.

Title: AI Data Scientist
Description:
About Exsolvæ

Exsolvæ is a Brussels-based Data & AI consultancy.

Business is structured around three main areas: Data Foundations, Data Science & AI, and BI & Analytics.




We build AI systems that run in production for Belgian enterprises across finance, pharma, insurance, retail, and energy.




We're looking for a Data Scientist specialised in Artificial Intelligence to design and ship these systems with us.




What we look for?

Technical depth: you've put AI into production and can defend your design choices.
Clear communication with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Business sense: you understand what decision a model serves before building it.




The Role:

You will design, build, and run AI systems for our clients across Belgium.

Hands-on building, in production, at scale.




What will you do?

Build end-to-end RAG solutions over enterprise data.
Integrate Model Context Protocol (MCP) to connect LLMs with internal tools and data.
Design multi-agent architectures to automate complex workflows.
Fine-tune and evaluate LLMs; implement guardrails for hallucination, toxicity, and relevance.
Deploy to production: API, monitoring, scaling.




The Technical Stack

LLMs: OpenAI GPT-4/4o, Azure OpenAI, Claude, LLaMA, Mistral, HuggingFace.
RAG: LangChain, LlamaIndex, vector stores (Pinecone, Weaviate, Chroma), Azure AI Search.
MCP / agents: Anthropic MCP, LangGraph, Semantic Kernel, AutoGen, CrewAI.
Classic ML: Scikit-learn, XGBoost, PyTorch, TensorFlow, MLflow.
Infra / deploy: Azure ML, Docker, Kubernetes, FastAPI, Streamlit.
Languages: Python (primary), SQL, TypeScript.




The Mindset we are looking for:

You ship production systems, not POCs.
You adapt your communication to your audience.
You stay rigorous: you don't overclaim.
You take ownership end to end.
You design for trust: evaluation and guardrails from the start.
You mentor through how you work.




Profile & Experience

3–8 years in Data Science, including 1–3 in Artificial Intelligence/LLM.

Mid to senior level.

Right to work in Belgium and Brussels mobility required.




Languages

Fluency in English is required, along with full professional proficiency in either French or Dutch.

Trilingual EN/FR/NL is a strong asset.




What makes you stand out?

Production Generative AI deployments you can point to.
A Master's/PhD in CS, mathematics, physics, or engineering.
Azure AI Engineer or Azure Data Scientist Associate certification.
Public work: open source, papers, conference talks.
Experience in a regulated vertical: finance, pharma, public sector.




What Exsolvæ Offer:

Transparent compensation, discussed from the first interview, plus our Solver Incentive Program.
Full focus on the work, no internal reporting overhead.
Engagements we can deliver well.
Real growth paths and time to publish or speak.




What this means for you:

Work on production Artificial Intelligence (RAG, agents and MCP). For clients serious about deploying it.
A €2,000 annual Knowledge & Networking budget for certifications and conferences.
A GenAI workspace and current tools from day one, through our OpenAI partnership.
Titles earned through demonstrated work.




Feel like a Solver? Reach out.

Title: Data Scientist - Banking
Description:
Data Scientist – Financial Services

About NORRIQ

NORRIQ is an international organization headquartered in Denmark and Belgium, with over 350 consultants dedicated to driving business transformation through innovative technology.

Our Financial Services (FS) practice includes 100+ specialists helping banks and financial institutions adapt to evolving client expectations, leverage cutting-edge technologies, and navigate increasingly complex regulatory environments.

At NORRIQ, we believe that people are our greatest asset. We foster a culture of innovation, growth, and excellence, offering tailored training, clear career paths, and a strong sense of community. Join us on this exciting journey and become a valued member of the NORRIQ family — connected for life!

The Opportunity

NORRIQ Financial Services is looking for a Data Scientist to accelerate our data analytics, machine learning, and generative AI initiatives. You will work with leading financial institutions to design, build, and deliver data-driven and AI-powered solutions that create tangible business impact.

Roles & Responsibilities

Client-Facing Responsibilities


Design and implement machine learning and generative AI solutions for complex financial use cases.
Conduct exploratory data analysis and build insightful dashboards or visualizations.
Present analytical findings and actionable recommendations to senior management and stakeholders.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Data Engineers, Business Analysts, Developers) to industrialize AI solutions.
Provide technical guidance and advisory on data science and AI best practices.


Technical Responsibilities


Develop and maintain AI and ML models using Python, scikit-learn, and deep learning frameworks.
Apply a wide range of analytical methodologies, including:
Generative AI techniques (Prompt Engineering, RAG, Fine-tuning, Speech-to-Text, Agentic Applications, Image Generation & Interpretation)
Predictive AI and classical machine learning approaches.
Contribute to production-grade AI applications using tools such as GitLab, DVC, and MLOps frameworks.
Ensure reproducibility, version control, and performance optimization across models and pipelines.
Create and maintain Jupyter notebooks for experimentation and documentation.

Internal Contributions


Contribute to NORRIQ’s AI & Data Science Center of Excellence through knowledge sharing and internal projects.
Support proposal writing and solution architecture for client engagements.
Stay at the forefront of developments in data science, AI, and GenAI frameworks (LangChain, Haystack, etc.).


Our Data Scientist Must Have


Fluency in English, and Dutch or French, with at least passive knowledge of the other
Belgian residency
At least few years of experience in a similar role.


What NORRIQ Offers You


Be part of a fast-growing, international organization with a unique and people-centered culture
Enjoy a highly competitive compensation package, including a company car, iPhone, and additional allowances
Access extensive training programs in Belgium and Denmark, focusing on both technical expertise and soft skills
Work with a dynamic and diverse team of experienced professionals and young talent in a collaborative environment within the financial services sector


Join NORRIQ to elevate your career, contribute to transformative projects, and make a lasting impact in the financial services industry!

  • At least few years of experience in a similar role.

What NORRIQ Offers You

  • Be part of a fast-growing, international organization with a unique and people-centered culture
  • Enjoy a highly competitive compensation package, including a company car, iPhone, and additional allowances
  • Access extensive training programs in Belgium and Denmark, focusing on both technical expertise and soft skills
  • Work with a dynamic and diverse team of experienced professionals and young talent in a collaborative environment within the financial services sector

Join NORRIQ to elevate your career, contribute to transformative projects, and make a lasting impact in the financial services industry!

Title: Data & AI Science Consultant
Description:
Are you willing to make a step in your career and deliver Analytics or AI products from early discovery stages up to industrialized solutions to re-imagine the future of the industry as part of large transformation projects?

If this is the case, this role will be of interest to you!

Team



Be at the forefront of the industry, you’ll create, own and make it a reality for clients looking to better serve their connected customers and operate always-on enterprises.

Become an integral part of our Data& AI team with the credibility, expertise and insight clients depend on.

Be working with famous brands and household names – no worrying about how to explain what you do to your family again!

Work alongside global teams to collaborate and deliver the best solutions for our clients.

Align with our industry partners to develop your career and the solutions we deliver for our clients.

Develop others by bringing your unique knowledge and skillsets to the wider practice.



Process



Drive and implement real world solutions into production that drive business value to our clients

Solve challenging business problems using advanced machine learning methods such as Deep Learning and quantitative analytics.

Understand business requirements and support the development of business cases.

Run discovery analytics to identify new and innovative opportunities.

Partner with developers and engineers to deploy machine learning algorithms to deliver business value.

Define approaches to embed and scale machine learning models.

Build reusable assets, solutions and develop best practices for current and future business problems.

Consult on complex analyses and advanced machine learning methods.

Communicate and provide guidance to senior client leadership and teams.



Data



Contribute data science expertise to new sales activities.

Shape DS Strategy and the delivery of DS solutions.

Operate across multiple industries and functions.

Develop others by bringing your unique knowledge and skillsets to the wider practice.



Your team

The Data and AI revolution is changing everything. It’s everywhere – transforming how we work and play. Join Accenture and help transform leading organisations and communities around the world. Accenture Data and AI Science is driving these exciting changes and bringing them to life across 40 industries in more than 120 countries. The sheer scale of our capabilities and client engagements and the way we collaborate with the ecosystem, operate and deliver value provides an unparalleled opportunity to grow and advance.

Accenture’s Data and AI practice covers the range of Data and AI skills, from Strategy, Data Science, Data Architecture, AI Engineering and Visual Insights. When combined with Accenture’s broader Strategy and Consulting practice, we are able to bring together the unique ability to drive end to end business change through the application of Data and AI.

You'll learn, grow and advance in an innovative culture that thrives on shared success, diverse ways of thinking and enables boundaryless opportunities that can drive your career in new and exciting ways.

If you’re looking for a challenging career working in a vibrant environment with access to training and a global network of experts, this could be the role for you. As part of our global team, you'll be working with cutting-edge technologies and will have the opportunity to develop a wide range of new skills on the job.

Accenture is a leading global professional services company, providing a broad range of services in strategy and consulting, interactive, technology and operations, with digital capabilities across all of these services. With our thought leadership and culture of innovation, we apply industry expertise, diverse skill sets and next-generation technology to each business challenge.

We believe in inclusion and diversity and supporting the whole person. Our core values comprise of Stewardship, Best People, Client Value Creation, One Global Network, Respect for the Individual and Integrity. Year after year, Accenture is recognized worldwide not just for business performance but for inclusion and diversity too.

“Across the globe, one thing is universally true of the people of Accenture: We care deeply about what we do and the impact we have with our clients and with the communities in which we work and live. It is personal to all of us.” – Julie Sweet, Accenture CEO

Your background



Relevant work experience in data science, machine learning, and business analytics

Practical experience in coding languages eg. Python, R, Scala, etc.; (Python preferred)

Proficiency in database technologies eg. SQL, ETL, No-SQL, DW, and Big Data technologies e.g. pySpark, Hive, etc.

Experienced working with structured and also unstructured data eg. Text, PDFs, jpgs, call recordings, video, etc.

Knowledge of machine learning modelling techniques and how to fine-tune those models eg. XGBoost, Deep Neural Networks, Transformers, Markov chains, etc.

Experience using specialized machine learning libraries e.g. Fastai, Keras, Tensorflow, pytorch, sci-kit learn, huggingface, etc.

Must demonstrate the capacity of reading, understanding and implementing new techniques in the field of machine learning as they emerge.

Experience of using Cloud technologies eg. AWS, GCP or Azure.

Specialised visualisation techniques eg. D3.js, ggplot etc.

Strong verbal/written communication & data presentation skills

Awareness of Large Language Models (LLMs) and a strong enthusiasm for continuous learning and staying updated with the latest advancements in the field

Fluent in English, in Dutch and/or in French



Bonus point If you…



Willingness and ability to learn quickly and apply creative thinking to finding great solutions and drive their implementation

Ability to successfully build relationships with clients and stakeholders

Mastery of problem solving and solutioning

Proven history and background in statistics/mathematics/macroeconomics



Our offer

Accenture is an incredible place to work - and keep learning. By joining us, you’ll become part of a global company with a world-class brand and reputation. Besides the work we do for our clients, we’re really proud of our vibrant, diverse workplace culture: we believe in openness and honesty, fairness and equality, common sense and realism. We want to get to know the real you and help you explore and grow - whatever it is you're great at. So you will always have lots of learning opportunities (formal and informal) to improve your role-specific skills and expertise.

Besides our high-profile, challenging projects and our nurturing work environment, we offer excellent employee benefits, including:



13th month + holiday allowance

20 + 12 holidays

Laptop and smartphone

Hospitalization insurance and extensive group insurance package

Bonus program

Training & learning opportunities

Green mobility program: e-bikes, public transport, bike 2 work allowance

Flex-rewards: decide on your rewards package with our flexible benefits tool

Discount program: get discounts at your favorite (online) shops

Employee shares purchase plan

Eco-cheques

And many more!

Title: AI & Data Engineer
Description:
SECO Group is looking for a AI & Automation Engineer.




We are seeking a motivated and skilled AI, Python & PowerApps Developer to join our digital transformation team. This hybrid role bridges traditional software development with low-code/no-code solutions, supporting business process automation, data analytics, and AI integration. The ideal candidate will have a strong foundation in Python development, experience with Microsoft Power Platform (especially PowerApps and Power Automate), and a keen interest in applying AI/ML solutions to real-world business problems.




Key responsabilities:

Python & AI Development

Design, develop, and maintain Python-based applications and scripts.
Build and deploy machine learning models using libraries such as NumPy, Pandas, scikit-learn, TensorFlow, or PyTorch.
Integrate AI solutions (e.g., NLP, predictive analytics, computer vision) into enterprise workflows.
Work with APIs and automation frameworks to connect disparate systems.




PowerApps Development

Design and build custom applications using Microsoft PowerApps.
Develop and automate workflows using Power Automate.
Integrate Power Platform apps with SharePoint, Teams, Dynamics 365, Dataverse, and other Microsoft services.
Ensure responsive and user-friendly UI/UX in PowerApps.




General

Collaborate with cross-functional teams including business analysts, project managers, and end users.
Document technical specifications and provide end-user training and support.
Stay current on emerging AI trends, Microsoft Power Platform updates, and relevant technologies.
Ensure security, performance, and scalability of all solutions.




Required Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
Proven experience in Python development.
Experience with machine learning, AI frameworks, or related projects.
Hands-on experience developing with Microsoft PowerApps and Power Automate.
Good understanding of data integration, REST APIs, and Microsoft 365 ecosystem.
Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Experience with Power BI and data visualization.
Familiarity with Azure AI services, Dataverse, and Microsoft Copilot integration.
Knowledge of DevOps practices and version control (e.g., Git).
Experience working in agile environments.
Microsoft certifications in Power Platform, Azure, or AI/ML (a plus).




Soft Skills:

Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
Ability to work independently and in a team environment.
Agile mindset and a passion for continuous learning and innovation.




SECO offers:

Opportunity to work on impactful digital transformation projects.
A collaborative and forward-thinking team environment.
Flexible work arrangements and competitive compensation.
Professional development and training opportunities.




Interested?

👇 Apply now and help build a safer future with SECO Belgium!

Send us your application via our website https://groupseco.be/careers/ or via mail HR_talent@groupseco.com (contact Yasmine Bouharaoui)!

Title: Specialist Tech – Data Scientist (M/V/X)
Description:
Werkomgeving

De Algemene Dienst Inlichting en Veiligheid (ADIV) – QUAERO ET TEGO: IK ONDERZOEK EN BESCHERM

De bedreigingen voor onze samenleving veranderen voortdurend. ADIV is de Belgische referentiedienst voor inlichtingen wat betreft Defensie en het buitenland. Met onze inlichtingen dragen we bij tot het beschermen van de belangen van Defensie, ons land en zijn bevolking. We hebben een diepgaand inzicht in de huidige en toekomstige uitdagingen op het gebied van inlichtingen, veiligheid en cyberspace. Dankzij een geïntegreerde aanpak, zowel nationaal als internationaal, werken we samen met onze verschillende partners om de interne veiligheid te handhaven en te versterken.

De Cyber Command is een cross-functionele eenheid die binnen Defensie zal groeien, terwijl het deel blijft uitmaken van ADIV, waarvan het het wettelijke kader en de missies deelt. Het heeft een dubbele rol. Cyber Command is verantwoordelijk voor de cyberbeveiliging van de netwerken en wapensystemen die door Defensie worden gebruikt, maar ook voor het verzamelen van informatie en het uitvoeren van operaties in cyberspace namens ADIV.

Binnen deze context bouwt het Tech Team van de Digital Influence Collection Unit (DICU) aan innovatieve oplossingen voor data-analyse, infrastructuur en cybersecurity. DICU steunt op twee pijlers:


Open Source Intelligence (OSINT): evolueert mee met moderne technologieën zoals machine learning en sociale media en vormt een essentiële pijler binnen het inlichtingenwerk.
Information Warfare (IW): bestrijdt desinformatie en fake news door geavanceerde technologieën in te zetten om online content te analyseren, te verifiëren en te lokaliseren – cruciaal in moderne conflicten.


Taakomschrijving

Als Specialist Tech – Data Scientist werkt u aan innovatieve oplossingen voor OSINT en cyberoperaties. Uw verantwoordelijkheden omvatten:


Voorbereiden en beheren van datasets (leaks, open source) en integreren in veilige databases
Ontwikkelen van intuïtieve interfaces (GUI) voor data-exploratie en gebruikersinteractie
Testen en implementeren van big data-technologieën zoals ELK Stack en graph databases (bv. ArangoDB)
Ontwerpen van cloudarchitecturen volgens best practices inzake veiligheid en schaalbaarheid
Opzetten van CI/CD pipelines en zero-trustoplossingen voor veilige en efficiënte ontwikkeling
Creëren van containeromgevingen (Docker, Kubernetes) voor flexibele en robuuste toepassingen
Ontwikkelen en onderhouden van anonieme netwerken en IT-infrastructuur voor veilige communicatie
Uitvoeren van risicoanalyses en security audits en definiëren van cybersecurity policies
Ondersteunen van OSINT- en IW-teams met technische expertise en probleemoplossing
Organiseren van trainingen en workshops en actief kennis delen binnen het team


Profielomschrijving


Je bent in het bezit van een Master- of Bachelordiploma in IT, of gelijkwaardig door ervaring.
Je bent vertrouwd met programmeertalen zoals Python en hebt kennis van big data-technologieën, cloudplatformen en containertechnologie.
Je hebt ervaring met data-analyse, modellering en visualisatie.
Je bent analytisch, oplossingsgericht en innovatief ingesteld.
Je beschikt over goede communicatieve vaardigheden en kan complexe materie helder uitleggen.
Je bent een teamplayer, maar kan ook zelfstandig werken.
Een degelijke kennis van het technisch Engels is onontbeerlijk.


Je hebt de Belgische nationaliteit en komt in aanmerking voor Belgische, NAVO- en EU-veiligheidsmachtiging “Zeer Geheim”.

Troeven


Goede kennis van het Frans (tweetalige werkomgeving).
Ervaring met OSINT, cybersecurity en risk management.
Certificaten in data science, cloud of cybersecurity.
Ervaring met CI/CD, zero trust en infrastructuurarchitectuur.


Arbeidsvoorwaarden

Als je geselecteerd wordt, moet je op de datum van indiensttreding voldoen aan de volgende voorwaarden om aan de slag te kunnen:


Belg zijn.
Burgerlijke en politieke rechten genieten.
Van goed gedrag zijn in overeenstemming met de vereisten van de betreffende functie.
In het bezit zijn van een veiligheidsmachtiging “Zeer Geheim” vereist door de functie of bereid zijn om een veiligheidsonderzoek “Zeer Geheim” te ondergaan alvorens in dienst te treden, met de verplichting om een positief resultaat te behalen, zo niet zou dit aanleiding geven tot uitsluiting uit de dienst (conform de wet van 11 december 1998 betreffende de classificatie en de veiligheidsmachtigingen, -attesten en -adviezen, gewijzigd door de wet van 03-05-2005).


Solliciteer je voor een functie binnen ADIV, wees dan discreet en beperk je communicatie hierover, ook binnen je onmiddellijke omgeving.

Wij bieden

Onze federale overheid is volop aan het informatiseren : van cybersecurity uitdagingen tot digitale communicatie, van netwerkverbeteringen om onze nieuwe manier van werken te ondersteunen tot complexe ontwikkelingsprojecten en optimalisatietrajecten van bestaande applicaties.

Wil je hieraan meewerken en zo een maatschappelijke meerwaarde creëren, terwijl je ook aan je persoonlijke ontwikkeling kunt werken en maximale mogelijkheden krijgt om je ambities waar te maken ? Dan bieden we je een duidelijk doel in een stimulerende omgeving en een zeer competitief salarispakket met een hele reeks extralegale voordelen (hospitalisatieverzekering, maaltijdcheques, mobiliteit, pensioenfonds,…).

Tevens hechten we veel belang aan een gezonde balans tussen werk en privé – met telewerk voor de meeste functies – en zijn onze kantoren zeer goed bereikbaar met het openbaar vervoer.

Solliciteer je voor een functie binnen ADIV, wees dan discreet en beperk je communicatie hierover, ook binnen je onmiddellijke omgeving.

Wij bieden

Onze federale overheid is volop aan het informatiseren : van cybersecurity uitdagingen tot digitale communicatie, van netwerkverbeteringen om onze nieuwe manier van werken te ondersteunen tot complexe ontwikkelingsprojecten en optimalisatietrajecten van bestaande applicaties.

Wil je hieraan meewerken en zo een maatschappelijke meerwaarde creëren, terwijl je ook aan je persoonlijke ontwikkeling kunt werken en maximale mogelijkheden krijgt om je ambities waar te maken ? Dan bieden we je een duidelijk doel in een stimulerende omgeving en een zeer competitief salarispakket met een hele reeks extralegale voordelen (hospitalisatieverzekering, maaltijdcheques, mobiliteit, pensioenfonds,…).

Tevens hechten we veel belang aan een gezonde balans tussen werk en privé – met telewerk voor de meeste functies – en zijn onze kantoren zeer goed bereikbaar met het openbaar vervoer.

Title: AI Engineer
Description:
Bij AI-D helpen we organisaties om artificiële intelligentie tastbaar en waardevol te maken.

Niet met buzzwords, maar met concrete oplossingen en strategisch advies dat werkt.

Voor ons is AI geen tijdelijke trend, maar een structurele verandering in hoe bedrijven denken en doen.




We bouwen oplossingen die impact maken: van slimme assistenten en procesautomatisatie tot op maat gemaakte analyses, decision support en AI-integraties. Onze projecten combineren technische expertise met businessinzicht en bieden een unieke omgeving om bij te leren, te experimenteren en impact te maken.




Over de rol




Als AI Engineer / Data Science Consultant bij AI-D werk je aan uitdagende AI-projecten bij klanten uit diverse sectoren. Je vertaalt complexe businessuitdagingen naar concrete AI-oplossingen met meetbare impact.


Taken



Ontwikkelen van AI-oplossingen in o.a. NLP, computer vision of optimalisatie


Bouwen en trainen van modellen (bv. met Python, PyTorch, LangChain, etc.)


Ontwikkelen en testen van AI-assistenten (spraak, chat, multimodaal)


Bouwen van automatisatieflows


Beheren van klantrelaties en presenteren van resultaten aan stakeholders


Kennis delen via workshops, demo's of events



Vereisten



Je hebt minstens 3 jaar relevante ervaring in data science of AI-consulting


Je bent vlot in Python en vertrouwd met libraries zoals PyTorch, NumPy of scikit-learn


Je hebt ervaring met cloudplatformen zoals Azure, AWS of GCP


Je denkt klantgericht, neemt ownership en communiceert helder


Je hebt een masterdiploma in een relevant domein (bv. Computerwetenschappen, Data Science, AI...)


Je spreekt en schrijft vlot Nederlands



Benefits



Je werkt in een jong, multidisciplinair team met impactvolle projecten


Je krijgt autonomie én ondersteuning om je rol vorm te geven


Je draagt bij aan de uitbouw van een snelgroeiend AI-bedrijf


Je kan meeschrijven aan de AI-toepassingen van morgen





Zin om bij te dragen aan slimme AI-oplossingen in een praktijkgerichte omgeving?

Stuur je cv of korte motivatie via Join.com. We nemen snel contact met je op voor een kennismaking.

Title: AI & Forecasting Data Consultant
Description:
Amaris Consulting is looking for a motivated AI & Forecasting Data Consultant to join our team in Brussels. You will contribute to the development of advanced analytics and AI solutions that enhance forecasting performance, automate anomaly detection, and support strategic planning decisions across complex business environments.




🔥 Your Responsibilities:

Design and develop machine learning models for forecasting and demand prediction
Build segmentation models to classify products, customers, and demand patterns
Develop anomaly detection mechanisms to identify forecasting inconsistencies and unusual trends
Analyze large datasets to generate actionable business insights
Support the development of AI-driven decision-support tools
Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand forecasting challenges and requirements
Evaluate model performance and continuously improve forecasting accuracy
Participate in proof-of-concepts and AI innovation initiatives
Document methodologies, assumptions, and model outputs
Ensure compliance with data governance and security requirements




🎯 Your Profile:

Master's degree in Data Science, Supply Chain, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field
3+ years of experience in data science, machine learning, predictive analytics, or forecasting
Strong knowledge of Python and machine learning libraries
Experience with forecasting models, time-series analysis, and predictive analytics
Knowledge of anomaly detection, clustering, segmentation, and classification techniques
Experience working with large datasets and data visualization tools
Understanding of supply chain, demand planning, or forecasting processes is considered a strong asset
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
Fluent in English
Experience with cloud, AI, or analytics platforms is a plus




🚀 What We Offer:

A truly international and multicultural work environment.
Access to training programs and certifications to accelerate your development.
The opportunity to propose and lead innovative ideas within the company.
Personalized coaching and mentoring from experienced professionals.
A tailor-made career path, aligned with your growth ambitions.




💡 Why Join Amaris Consulting?

Agility: Work in a dynamic, flexible, and stimulating environment.
International exposure: Collaborate with teams in over 60 countries and enjoy mobility opportunities.
Intrapreneurship: Take ownership of your ideas or create your own Business Unit.
Supportive management: Your manager will guide you based on your individual profile and goals.




Amaris Consulting is proud to be an equal-opportunity workplace. We are committed to promoting diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. For this purpose, we welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age, marital status, disability, or other characteristics.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Expleo is your transformative partner in technological innovation. As a cutting-edge global engineering, technology, and consulting service provider, we empower talented professionals to drive groundbreaking solutions and shape the future of business. With a smart blend of bold thinking and reliable execution, we’re able to fast-track innovation through each step of your value chain.

We are strategically positioned to build value, with a global footprint across 30 countries. We are as global and local as you need us to be, with strong best-in-class pan-European technological centers and unique best-shoring capabilities.

We leverage a network of high value-adding affiliates in consulting and industrial excellence, and leading partners across multiple sectors to provide you with the most comprehensive services and solutions in an ever-changing environment.

Responsibilities
Identify, analyze, and translate business needs and challenges into AI- and data-driven solutions
Collect, clean, and prepare data for analysis, modeling, and experimentation
Design, develop, and deploy machine learning and Generative AI models
Integrate AI solutions into digital products, APIs, and automated workflows
Build, operate, and maintain MLOps pipelines for model training, deployment, monitoring, and lifecycle management
Pioneer innovative approaches through advanced prompt engineering, state-of-the-art fine-tuning techniques, and cutting-edge LLM orchestration tools
Evaluate and continuously optimize models for performance, scalability, reliability, and business value
Ensure ethical AI practices, data governance, and compliance with regulatory and industry standards
Communicate findings, insights, and recommendations clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Be at the forefront of technological evolution by continuously exploring, researching, and mastering emerging trends, revolutionary tools, and industry-defining best practices in AI, machine learning, generative AI, and advanced data science technologies
Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams and key stakeholders to deliver impactful business outcomes


Qualifications
3+ years of proven experience in machine learning, AI engineering, or data science
Advanced proficiency in Python, SQL, and cutting-edge AI/ML frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn), with a proven track record of innovative implementations
Experience with MLOps tools (MLflow, Docker, FastAPI, Airflow, CI/CD pipelines, or cloud ML services)
Proven experience with enterprise-scale cloud ecosystems and advanced cloud AI/ML services (Microsoft Azure, Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud Platform)
Solid knowledge of data engineering and software engineering fundamentals
Familiarity with LLMs, vector databases, and prompt design / orchestration frameworks
Experience with data visualisation tools (Power BI, Tableau, or Matplotlib)
Strong analytical mindset and ability to bridge technical and business needs
Exceptional ability to communicate complex technical concepts clearly and demonstrate independent, creative problem-solving skills in high-pressure environments
Fluent in FR or NL + strong command of English


#LM-01

Benefits

At Expleo, we are committed to providing a competitive salary and substantial benefits offering with unique wellbeing, financial and employee recognition programs.

We pride ourselves on our diverse and inclusive culture and offer a variety of family-friendly policies and enhanced leave to help you achieve a healthy work life balance.

By providing significant learning and development opportunities, we encourage individual growth and success throughout your career with us.


Collaborative working environment – we stand shoulder to shoulder with our clients and our peers through good times and challenges
We empower all passionate technology-loving professionals by allowing them to expand their skills and take part in inspiring projects
Expleo Academy - enables you to acquire and develop the right skills by delivering a suite of accredited training courses
Enjoy a comprehensive benefits package, including hospitalization and outpatient insurance, a pension and group insurance plan, employee wellbeing initiatives, sports and social events, gifts for special occasions, and much more!
Enjoy meal and eco vouchers to support your daily needs and sustainable purchases
Receive monthly representation fees to support work-related expenses
32 vacation days (20 + 12 for a 40-hour week).
Always working as one team, our people are not afraid to think big and question the status quo.
Flexible homeworking policy – Balance your career and personal life


Our Commitment to Diversity

“We are an equal opportunities employer and welcome applications from all suitably qualified persons regardless of their race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation or age”

Title: AI & Data Engineer
Description:
What You'll Do

Are you the AI & Data Engineer who wants to join our team to develop innovative data pipelines and state-of-the-art AI solutions?

As an AI & Data Engineer at iO, you are first and foremost an experienced software engineer who builds, integrates, and operationalizes AI and data solutions within clients’ existing IT ecosystems.

You will work on a wide variety of AI challenges: from intelligently leveraging or fine-tuning Large Language Models, agent and multi-agent setups, and applying RAG patterns, to building ETL pipelines that feed a data platform. A recurring pattern in your work is integrating existing cloud services and APIs into an efficient, scalable, and reliable solution. The focus lies on building production-grade systems that function correctly within complex, often enterprise-level IT landscapes.

Prompt engineering is an art form to you. You think creatively about how to achieve the best results, while paying attention to preventing hallucinations and implementing smart guardrails. You evaluate your solutions systematically to gain insight into output quality.

In your work, you will encounter many forms of AI: from statistical models to Large Language Models, from Generative AI in the domains of image, video, and audio to Natural Language Processing. The emphasis is mainly on applying and integrating existing models and services, and building the infrastructure around them.

You will work on a wide range of client challenges: from automatically analyzing documents to detecting events in audio or video data, building agents and orchestration solutions, generating graphical output based on data, or creating and validating content. Wherever AI can add value, you design and implement the complete solution as an engineer.

You collaborate with various AI experts within iO and with technical teams on the client side. Your solutions are based on modern (cloud) platforms, SaaS services, and programming languages such as TypeScript and Python.

What Are Your Responsibilities as an AI & Data Engineer?


Designing AI & Data solutions: Analyze client challenges related to AI and data and define solutions, approaches, and architecture
Software engineering & integration: Develop backend components, APIs, and services to ensure AI solutions integrate seamlessly with existing applications and platforms
Data Pipeline Development: Design and build robust ETL/ELT processes and real-time data streams
Data Platform Architecture: Set up modern data warehouses, data lakes, and analytics platforms
AI Integration: Implement machine learning pipelines and integrate AI services into data workflows
Data Quality & Governance: Ensure data quality, security, and compliance within data ecosystems
Performance Optimization: Optimize data processing performance and scalability
Cloud Data Services: Work with cloud-native data services such as AWS Glue, Azure Data Factory, or Google Dataflow
Monitoring & Observability: Implement monitoring and alerting for data pipelines and AI workloads


Your Qualities And Skills As AI & Data Engineer


You hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree with a focus on software engineering
You have several years of experience as a software engineer and have worked on data- and/or AI-driven systems in a professional, preferably enterprise, environment
You have experience with different LLM architectures and understand the strengths and weaknesses of various AI solutions
You are familiar with the principles of modern data architectures, data modeling, and big data processing
You are proficient in modern data processing languages such as Python and SQL, and have experience with frameworks such as Apache Spark, Pandas, or Dask
You are familiar with AWS or Azure cloud data platforms
You have a strong foundation in software engineering, including knowledge of CI/CD, unit testing, Git (flow), secure development, error handling, asynchronous operations, and real-time data streams
You are able to translate your ideas into technical architecture diagrams, data flow schemas, and documentation, while also clearly explaining your solutions to less technical stakeholders (clients and colleagues)
You enjoy sparring with colleagues about ideas and solutions and working collaboratively to achieve better results


What’s in it for you

Vacation icon Vacation icon used in website

32 vacation days

Based on a 40-hour work week, you’ll have 20 vacation days and 12 compensatory days of leave (reduction of working time).

Money icon Money icon used in website

Mobility & end-of-year bonus

After working at iO for 6 months, you can count on an end-of-year bonus. And in terms of mobility, you can choose from various options, depending on your job or preference.

SafeEnvironment icon Safe Environment icon used in website

Hospitalisation insurance

When something unfortunate happens, the last thing you need is the added stress of unexpected costs. That’s why all employees get hospitalisation insurance at Axa.

HybridWorking icon Hybrid Working icon used in website

Hybrid working

A good work-life balance is super important. Together with your team, you’ll decide when and where you work.

FoodBenefits icon Food Benefits icon used in website

Healthy snacks & lunch

At every iO campus, you can grab a healthy snack throughout the day and have a healthy and varied lunch for a small fee.

TrainingBudget icon Training Budget icon used in website

Personal training budget

Each year, you’ll get a budget of €3,000 to develop yourself, in your profession or as a person, through training, events, or courses.

Celebrations icon Celebrations icon used in website

Celebrating together

If there’s something to celebrate, we know how to party! From time to time, the party team at your campus will organise the best (theme) parties.
