Title: Senior Data Scientist
Description:
Position Description

(Note: This job posting is only open to resident in Belgium , native French or Dutch . If this is not the case, please have a look at our other job postings instead!)

Do you enjoy working with data?

Are you skilled at identifying and solving problems? Are you comfortable using a variety of techniques in a fast-changing environment? Join our growing multidisciplinary innovation lab as a Senior Data Scientist.

Your role in our team

You support our clients within an innovation ecosystem and help shape their future data-driven strategies. You provide actionable insights using the latest concepts from the field of artificial intelligence. You develop machine learning algorithms that address current technological challenges. You also help clients visualize the information you generate, making it easy to share with stakeholders.

Thanks to your experience, you can translate the latest industry and IT trends into innovative solutions tailored to our clients’ operations. You participate in business development and sales processes to create new opportunities with existing or potential CGI clients. You are a team player who thrives in diverse environments and collaborates with other experts to find the best solutions.

How You Strengthen Our Team

You are proactive and enjoy taking on challenges. With your expertise, you deliver the best solutions and identify those that offer the most value. You are also capable of mentoring and coaching your colleagues. You bring the following skills and experience:

A university degree (Bachelor or Master) with a strong statistical component, such as Applied Mathematics, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, or Econometrics.

Proficiency in Python and/or R (pandas, tensorflow, scikit-learn, matplotlib).

Experience with advanced analytics, big data, and machine learning.

Knowledge of ML libraries for classification and regression (scikit-learn, keras, pytorch).

Familiarity with NLP libraries (SpaCy, NLPK, Stanford).

Proven experience solving complex challenges through data analysis and modeling.

At least 5 years of experience in the data domain.

Knowledge of Agile working methodologies.

Diplôme universitaire (Bachelor ou Master) avec une forte composante statistique, tel que mathématiques appliquées, Data Science, Intelligence Artificielle, Informatique ou Économétrie.

Maîtrise de Python et/ou R (pandas, tensorflow, scikit-learn, matplotlib).

Expérience en analytics avancés, big data et machine learning.

Connaissance des bibliothèques ML pour la classification et la régression (scikit-learn, keras, pytorch).

Title: Medior Data Scientist
Company: Projective Talent | Part of Projective Group
Description:
You know how to build models. Now you want them to survive contact with reality.




If you’re a Data Scientist with a few years of experience, chances are you’re at a crossroads:

You don’t want to stay stuck in PoCs or isolated analyses
You want your AI solutions in production, used by real people
You want to grow technically and be taken seriously by the business




This role is built exactly for that next step.




The challenge

You’ll join a large, established AI organisation within a leading financial environment where AI is already embedded in daily operations. This is not an experimental lab, it’s a place where AI must be robust, explainable, scalable and responsibly deployed.




Here, Data Scientists are expected to:

Take ownership
Work with complex data and real constraints
Deliver solutions that last




What you’ll actually be working on

As a medior Data Scientist, you’ll translate concrete business challenges into production-grade AI solutions with visible impact.

You will:




Design and develop AI applications such as:

Virtual assistants enhancing customer experience
Advanced AI tools supporting employee efficiency and creativity
Intelligent automation of internal processes




Work across generative and classical AI, including:

Large Language Models (prompt engineering, RAG, fine-tuning)
Speech and multimodal AI
Agent-based AI systems
Statistical and machine learning techniques




The environment

A large AI tribe with experienced Data Scientists, AI Engineers and product roles
Agile teams where ownership, collaboration and pragmatism are the norm
An innovation-minded culture with direct communication and little politics
Access to one of the richest and most diverse data landscapes in the market




Who this role is for

This role is a strong fit if you:

Hold a Master’s degree in data science, statistics, computer science, engineering or a related quantitative field
Have around 3 or more years of experience as a Data Scientist or in a similar AI-focused role
Have deep expertise in at least one AI domain (e.g. NLP, ML, generative AI) and the ambition to broaden further
Are comfortable coding in Python and care about clean, maintainable work
Understand ML engineering basics (version control, reproducibility, CI/CD)
Communicate fluently in English, and have are fluent in French and/or Dutch

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
I'm currently working with an innovative company at the forefront of the AgriTech sector, leveraging AI and machine learning to transform the future of farming and sustainable food production. They are looking to hire a Machine Learning Engineer to help build intelligent solutions that improve crop yields, optimise resource usage, and drive greater efficiency across agricultural operations.




This is an opportunity to apply cutting-edge machine learning techniques to real-world challenges, helping shape a more sustainable and data-driven farming industry.

The Role




As a Machine Learning Engineer, you'll play a key role in developing and deploying production-grade ML solutions that support precision agriculture and smart farming initiatives. You'll work alongside software engineers, data scientists, and domain experts to deliver scalable systems capable of processing large volumes of sensor, satellite, and operational data.




Responsibilities

Design, build, and deploy machine learning models into production environments.
Develop data pipelines capable of processing large-scale agricultural and environmental datasets.
Build predictive models to improve crop health, yield forecasting, irrigation optimisation, and resource management.
Work with structured and unstructured data from IoT devices, sensors, drones, and satellite imagery.
Collaborate with engineering and product teams to translate business challenges into AI-driven solutions.
Implement MLOps best practices, including model monitoring, retraining, and automated deployment.
Explore emerging technologies including Generative AI and advanced analytics to enhance farming operations.




What They're Looking For

Essential Experience

3+ years of commercial experience building machine learning solutions.
Strong Python development skills.
Experience with PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, or similar ML frameworks.
Strong understanding of feature engineering, model evaluation, and optimisation techniques.
Experience working with large datasets and SQL databases.
Familiarity with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP.
Experience with Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to work within cross-functional teams.

Desirable

Experience working with geospatial data, satellite imagery, computer vision, or IoT datasets.
Exposure to time-series forecasting and predictive analytics.
Experience with Generative AI, LLMs, or MLOps platforms such as MLflow or Kubeflow.
Knowledge of distributed computing frameworks such as Spark or Ray.




Why Join?

Agriculture is undergoing a technological revolution, and my client is leading that change. Their mission is to empower farmers with intelligent tools that increase productivity, reduce waste, and support more sustainable food production.

This is an opportunity to work on meaningful problems where your models will have a tangible impact—not only on business performance but on the future of global agriculture.




If you're a Machine Learning Engineer looking to apply AI to one of the world's most essential industries, I'd be keen to speak with you.

Title: Data Full Stack
Description:
Health can’t wait.

Not for symptoms to get worse. Not for a six‑month appointment. Not for a system to catch up. But that’s exactly how healthcare works today. You wait, until you can’t.

Alan exists to end the wait.

Health is a universal right, and we believe this right can only become real when it’s coupled with prevention. We need to stop treating health as something we repair and start treating it as something we build, every day. It’s not solely a question of willpower. It’s the healthcare system itself that needs to work for everyone, in a sustainable way.

So we are building the new standard in prevention insurance. Alan is the first company that integrates insurance, prevention, and care into a single, acclaimed user experience.

We are on an incredible journey to build a global leading company, with a unique culture. We already partner with 40K+ companies of all sizes, serving more than 1M+ members, and have reached €800M+ in ARR.

Prevention as the new norm. That's what we're building with our team of 800+ people. If it speaks to you: we're hiring across France, Spain, Belgium, and Canada. And beyond.

⭐️ The Data Team


Being part of Alan's data team means you'll have a positive impact on people's health and wellbeing.
We create insights to help our colleagues focus on what is most impactful.
We build features and tools powered by data that directly help our members.
Examples include:
going deep into health consumption patterns,
understanding our product usage,
creating visibility to help our sales team perform,
automating how we reimburse our members,
building products that help our members stay healthy...

We also believe in having a full stack approach:


Each one of us contributes to all 3 aspects of the data spectrum (analytics, engineering, science). This encourages strong ownership and drives impact.
We own our infrastructure. We pride ourselves on a nimble data stack that gives us the flexibility we need. We're mainly using SQL, Python, R, Airflow, Snowflake, and Metabase.
We build in-house tooling to organize our data and serve it to the rest of the company.


🎯 Expected Skills And Abilities



You would thrive at Alan if most of this sounds like you:


Passionate about owning ambitious problems and developing simple solutions for them
Demonstrated impact during a first successful experience.

HTML:
Health can’t wait.

Not for symptoms to get worse. Not for a six‑month appointment. Not for a system to catch up. But that’s exactly how healthcare works today. You wait, until you can’t.

Alan exists to end the wait.

Health is a universal right, and we believe this right can only become real when it’s coupled with prevention. We need to stop treating health as something we repair and start treating it as something we build, every day. It’s not solely a question of willpower. It’s the healthcare system itself that needs to work for everyone, in a sustainable way.

So we are building the new standard in prevention insurance. Alan is the first company that integrates insurance, prevention, and care into a single, acclaimed user experience.

We are on an incredible journey to build a global leading company, with a unique culture. We already partner with 40K+ companies of all sizes, serving more than 1M+ members, and have reached €800M+ in ARR.

Prevention as the new norm. That's what we're building with our team of 800+ people. If it speaks to you: we're hiring across France, Spain, Belgium, and Canada. And beyond.

⭐️ The Data Team

  • Being part of Alan's data team means you'll have a positive impact on people's health and wellbeing.
  • We create insights to help our colleagues focus on what is most impactful.
  • We build features and tools powered by data that directly help our members.
  • Examples include:
    • going deep into health consumption patterns,
    • understanding our product usage,
    • creating visibility to help our sales team perform,
    • automating how we reimburse our members,
    • building products that help our members stay healthy...
We also believe in having a full stack approach:

  • Each one of us contributes to all 3 aspects of the data spectrum (analytics, engineering, science). This encourages strong ownership and drives impact.
  • We own our infrastructure. We pride ourselves on a nimble data stack that gives us the flexibility we need. We're mainly using SQL, Python, R, Airflow, Snowflake, and Metabase.
  • We build in-house tooling to organize our data and serve it to the rest of the company.

🎯 Expected Skills And Abilities



You would thrive at Alan if most of this sounds like you:

  • Passionate about owning ambitious problems and developing simple solutions for them
  • Demonstrated impact during a first successful experience.
  • Experience or willingness to grow in all 3 aspects of the data spectrum (analytics, engineering, science) -- being specialized in one of these areas is good too!

Title: Junior Data Scientist
Description:
Bij Christeyns zetten we data om in waardevolle inzichten. Als internationale B2B-speler in de hygiënechemie investeren we sterk in een toekomstgerichte data-omgeving die onze bedrijfsprocessen en duurzaamheidsambities ondersteunt.




Om ons data team verder te versterken, zoeken we een enthousiaste Junior Data Scientist. In deze rol krijg je de kans om je analytische vaardigheden verder te ontwikkelen en stap voor stap door te groeien naar een functie als Business Analyst. Je werkt mee aan projecten die een directe impact hebben op de werking en besluitvorming binnen onze internationale organisatie.




Jouw functie

Je vertaalt ruwe en complexe data uit verschillende interne en externe bronnen naar heldere, bruikbare inzichten voor proceseigenaars en management, zodat beslissingen onderbouwd en datagedreven kunnen worden genomen.
Je ontwerpt, ontwikkelt en onderhoudt dashboards, datamodellen en API’s die bedrijfsprocessen meetbaar, stuurbaar en transparant maken binnen een internationale organisatie.
Je werkt actief mee aan de verdere uitbouw en optimalisatie van de data-architectuur en -infrastructuur, waarbij betrouwbaarheid, performantie en schaalbaarheid centraal staan.
Je ontsluit data via gebruiksvriendelijke visualisaties en rapporteringen in tools zoals Power BI en Grafana, ondersteund door Databricks en Microsoft Azure.
Je neemt verantwoordelijkheid voor duurzaamheids- en ESG-gerelateerde data en zorgt voor correcte definities, consistente datasets en duidelijke rapportering ter ondersteuning van strategische doelstellingen.
Waar relevant pas je machine learning en geavanceerde analysetechnieken toe om patronen te identificeren, inzichten te verdiepen en data verder te verrijken.
Je stemt noden en verwachtingen rond data, visualisatie en voorspellende modellen af met proceseigenaars en werkt hiervoor nauw samen met IT en het data team.
Je rapporteert aan de Data Team Lead




Jouw profiel

Bachelor of Master in Data Science, Toegepaste Informatica, Handelsingenieur, Economie of een vergelijkbare richting;
Je bent een starter of hebt een eerste relevante ervaring via stage, studentenjob of een eerste werkervaring;
Interesse in data-analyse, bedrijfsprocessen en business intelligence;
Basiskennis van SQL, Power BI of Python is een pluspunt;
Interesse om door te groeien richting Business Analytics;
Zeer goede kennis van Nederlands en Engels.




Ons aanbod

Een uitdagende startersfunctie binnen een internationale en groeiende organisatie;
Begeleiding en opleiding door ervaren collega's;
Werken in een jong en dynamisch data team;
Ruimte voor initiatief, persoonlijke ontwikkeling en professionele groei;
Een marktconform salarispakket met extralegale voordelen.




Wie zijn wij?

Christeyns is een snelgroeiende Belgische hygiënespecialist in het B2B-segment met hoofdzetel in Gent. In iets meer dan 79 jaar evolueerde het bedrijf van een lokaal familiebedrijf tot een gerenommeerde wereldspeler in de internationale markt van chemicaliën en detergenten, met tevreden klanten in meer dan 50 landen.

De verschillende domeinen van de bedrijfsactiviteiten omvatten professionele textielverzorging, de voedings- en drankenindustrie, professionele reiniging, life sciences en gezondheidszorg. Christeyns onderscheidt zich door een permanente focus op klantgerichtheid, innovatie en ontwikkeling. Meer dan 1.000 medewerkers verspreid over meer dan 44 dochterondernemingen dragen dagelijks bij aan de verdere groei van de groep.




Interesse?

Wil je je carrière starten in data en analytics en doorgroeien naar een Business Analyst-rol binnen een internationale organisatie?




Stuur je cv en motivatie naar jobs@christeyns.com. We kijken ernaar uit om je te leren kennen.

Title: Data Scientist (NLP & Graph AI) (French Speaking)
Description:
We are looking for a talented Data Scientist with a strong focus on NLP and graph-based data to build intelligent systems for financial applications. This role will involve working with cutting-edge embedding models, LLMs and multilingual data.

Responsibilities

Develop and deploy NLP models for tasks such as text classification, entity recognition, and semantic search.
Work with graph data structures to model relationships and extract insights.
Build and optimize systems using embedding models and LLMs.
Design and implement Generative AI solutions for financial use cases.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver scalable data products.
Handle multilingual datasets, particularly in French and English.

Qualifications

Native French Speaker
2+ years of professional experience building and applying NLP or AI solutions in real-world or production environments
Strong experience in Natural Language Processing (NLP).
Experience working with graph-based data and graph algorithms.
Hands-on experience with embedding models, LLMs, and Generative AI.
Fluency in French and English (both required).
Proficiency in Python.
Experience with SQL and database systems.
Familiarity with knowledge graphs and vector databases.
Additional programming languages are a plus.




Company Overview

At SBS, we’re more than just a technology company, we’re a global fintech partner helping banks and financial institutions transform, innovate, and grow. With over 5,000 employees worldwide and clients in more than 80 countries, our solutions power everything from digital banking and lending to payments and core banking systems.




As part of 74Software, we’re backed by a group of leading software companies delivering mission-critical solutions worldwide. Our focus is on delivering long-term value, leveraging cutting-edge technology, and fostering strong client partnerships. Join us and be part of a collaborative, forward-thinking team shaping the future of finance.

Why SBS?

At SBS, we’re committed to supporting our employees in every aspect of their lives, from health and wellbeing to financial security and lifestyle perks.

We offer benefits that allow you to thrive at work and beyond, including health coverage, retirement plans, paid time off, flexible work, career development, competitive pay, and global culture perks.

Equal Opportunity Employer

We are committed to providing equal opportunities and ensuring a fair and inclusive recruitment process. We do not discriminate on the basis of age, disability, gender, sexual orientation, race, religion, or any other protected characteristic.

Title: Research Software Engineer
Description:
To strengthen VITO’s Environmental Intelligence unit, we are looking for several Research Software Engineers. You will help shape the next generation of GeoAI services, applying advances in artificial intelligence, foundation models and large-scale Earth observation processing. Our platforms support the European Commission, the European Space Agency and regional public authorities in their decisions on climate, food security, biodiversity and infrastructure.




We are looking for highly skilled software engineers and applied scientists with a broad foundation in data engineering and data science. The exact balance between platform engineering, large-scale data processing and applied machine learning will be shaped by your expertise and by project needs. Our end products must not only be innovative but also ready for trusted delivery—operable in regulated or access-restricted settings with strong requirements on robustness, traceability and information assurance. We therefore welcome applications from different profiles, including candidates who are stronger in some areas than others.




You join a talented team that operates at the forefront of European Space programmes, public policy and applied research. You work on systems with global visibility and concrete impact, with the freedom to bring your own ideas into platforms that are used well beyond our institute. We offer a working environment that combines the rigour of a research organisation with the pace of a modern software team.




Your responsibilities:




Design, build and operate software components for geospatial AI, ranging from data ingestion and processing pipelines to interactive analytical applications and machine learning workflows.

Develop and extend cloud-based platforms such as the Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem and Terrascope, contributing to open standards such as openEO and reusable building blocks.

Apply state-of-the-art artificial intelligence techniques to Earth observation, including deep learning, geospatial foundation models and agentic systems that combine reasoning with geospatial data and tools.

Translate requirements from thematic experts, end users and government stakeholders into robust, scalable and maintainable software.

Apply engineering practices such as continuous integration, automated testing, infrastructure as code and machine learning operations to keep services reliable and efficient.

Follow developments in software engineering, artificial intelligence and remote sensing closely, and bring relevant innovations into our platforms.

Job Requirements:

Master’s or PhD degree in computer science, engineering, physics, mathematics or an equivalent discipline.

Solid software engineering background, with strong skills in Python and/or Scala and good familiarity with modern development practices, version control, testing and code review.

Experience with distributed processing frameworks in cloud or HPC environments is a plus.

Working knowledge of machine learning and deep learning frameworks, and interest in applying them to geospatial or sensor data.

Ability to operate in an international, multidisciplinary environment, with excellent command of English.

A self-organized attitude, strong analytical skills and the communication skills needed to engage technical and non-technical stakeholders.




Considered as a large plus:




Knowledge of the Dutch language

Experience with Earth observation data, in particular the Copernicus Sentinel missions, Synthetic Aperture Radar, hyperspectral or very high-resolution imagery.

Prior experience in classified information handling or accredited environments.

Contributions to open-source geospatial projects, open standards such as those of the Open Geospatial Consortium

Experience with geospatial foundation models, large language models applied to geospatial workflows, or model serving at scale.

Experience with secure software development, supply chain security and zero-trust architecture.







Offer




We operate in an activity‑based way, meaning your work activities guide where you work. Our headquarters is located in Mol, and we anticipate an average onsite presence of about 50%. We also support flexibility: part‑time remote work or working from one of our satellite offices in Antwerp‑Berchem, Genk, Leuven, Kortrijk or Ostend is possible.

A competitive salary with a range of benefits, including allowances, insurance, and a modular package of holidays.

Innovation is our asset, so it goes without saying that we give our employees the opportunity to take additional training courses and stay up-to-date in their field. You will always have the opportunity to broaden your knowledge and discover new technologies.

You will have the opportunity to be part of an organization with an international reputation, known for its cutting-edge technological research and scientific consultancy.

With us, you will contribute directly to sustainable developments at local, national and global level through your innovative applications. You provide answers to contemporary societal challenges.

Moreover, we highly value a good work-life balance at VITO! This is achieved by adopting a flexible attitude depending on your specific needs.

  • Apply engineering practices such as continuous integration, automated testing, infrastructure as code and machine learning operations to keep services reliable and efficient.

  • Follow developments in software engineering, artificial intelligence and remote sensing closely, and bring relevant innovations into our platforms.

Job Requirements:

  • Master’s or PhD degree in computer science, engineering, physics, mathematics or an equivalent discipline.

  • Solid software engineering background, with strong skills in Python and/or Scala and good familiarity with modern development practices, version control, testing and code review.

  • Experience with distributed processing frameworks in cloud or HPC environments is a plus.

  • Working knowledge of machine learning and deep learning frameworks, and interest in applying them to geospatial or sensor data.

  • Ability to operate in an international, multidisciplinary environment, with excellent command of English.

  • A self-organized attitude, strong analytical skills and the communication skills needed to engage technical and non-technical stakeholders.


Considered as a large plus:


  • Knowledge of the Dutch language

  • Experience with Earth observation data, in particular the Copernicus Sentinel missions, Synthetic Aperture Radar, hyperspectral or very high-resolution imagery.

  • Prior experience in classified information handling or accredited environments.

  • Contributions to open-source geospatial projects, open standards such as those of the Open Geospatial Consortium

  • Experience with geospatial foundation models, large language models applied to geospatial workflows, or model serving at scale.

  • Experience with secure software development, supply chain security and zero-trust architecture.



Offer


  • We operate in an activity‑based way, meaning your work activities guide where you work. Our headquarters is located in Mol, and we anticipate an average onsite presence of about 50%. We also support flexibility: part‑time remote work or working from one of our satellite offices in Antwerp‑Berchem, Genk, Leuven, Kortrijk or Ostend is possible.

  • A competitive salary with a range of benefits, including allowances, insurance, and a modular package of holidays.

  • Innovation is our asset, so it goes without saying that we give our employees the opportunity to take additional training courses and stay up-to-date in their field. You will always have the opportunity to broaden your knowledge and discover new technologies.

  • You will have the opportunity to be part of an organization with an international reputation, known for its cutting-edge technological research and scientific consultancy.

  • With us, you will contribute directly to sustainable developments at local, national and global level through your innovative applications. You provide answers to contemporary societal challenges.

  • Moreover, we highly value a good work-life balance at VITO! This is achieved by adopting a flexible attitude depending on your specific needs.

Title: AI/ML Engineer
Description:
🤖 The Job

At Dataroots, we deliver end-to-end data and AI solutions, from strategy and platform design to data engineering and AI-enabled systems. That broad scope is reflected in our team: AI/ML engineers, data engineers, cloud architects, and data strategists. Each discipline brings its own expertise, but we share knowledge, perspectives, and a common way of thinking across disciplines.

Within that team, you design, build and deploy AI solutions that deliver tangible business value for our clients across a variety of industries, spanning classical ML, modern GenAI, and Agentic AI use cases. Your work goes beyond getting the models to work; you help bring AI systems into production in a robust and scalable way, applying best practices in MLOps and LLMOps to ensure they are reliable, maintainable, and adaptable over time.




💼 What we’re looking for

You bring the following qualities & experiences to the role:

A deep passion for Artificial Intelligence and its applications.
A minimum of 2 years of experience developing AI-driven applications or systems.
Strong foundation in software design principles; Proficiency in Python & SQL is a must.
A solid understanding of core machine learning techniques and how to apply them pragmatically in production settings.
Familiarity with modern generative AI and agentic approaches, including retrieval-based systems and LLM orchestration patterns.
Experience deploying AI systems into production, with a solid grasp of model lifecycle management and cloud-native deployment practices.
Experience with at least one major cloud platform (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and a solid understanding of cloud-based AI and data workflows.
Front-end experience is considered a plus.
Strong communication skills and the ability to simplify complex technical concepts for clients.
A pragmatic, business-oriented mindset: able to translate business problems into technical scopes and help AI solutions land, with a focus on product thinking and real business value.
Fluency in English and Dutch.




Not every box needs to be ticked to be a great fit. If you’re excited about the role and eager to grow, we’d still love to hear from you.




🎉 Why choose Dataroots?

We’re a data & AI consulting company where people, growth, and impact go hand in hand. Since our early days, we’ve built a culture that values collaboration, knowledge sharing, and an environment where individuals can thrive. This focus has allowed us to grow steadily while staying true to what matters: delivering meaningful results for our clients and creating a place where people enjoy doing their best work.

Working at Dataroots means gaining exposure to a wide range of industries, challenges, and technologies. You’ll have the opportunity to step outside your comfort zone, while being supported by experienced colleagues who are always ready to share their expertise.

As part of the Talan Group, we further extend these opportunities by offering an international dimension to your career path.




💰 The Offer

An attractive salary with extralegal benefits, including:

+ Mobility budget or a company car with fuel/charging card

+ Hospitalisation and group insurance

+ High-end laptop

+ Smartphone with subscription

+ Substantial amount of holidays

+ Meal vouchers

+ ...

Diverse and welcoming work environment, where you’ll collaborate & unwind with colleagues from different cultures and disciplines. The organization of both fun & professional events and initiatives is actively encouraged and supported.
A training budget for individual and team learning opportunities.
Tons of team-building events and sports initiatives to stay connected and unwind.




📍 Where you’ll work

Our main offices are in Leuven and Ghent. These spaces are perfect for both focusing and unwinding with colleagues, as well as brainstorming sessions and team events.

While much of your work can be performed remotely, you will also work on-site with clients.




🎯 Ready to join Dataroots?

Excited to shape the future of AI with us? We’re looking forward to hearing from you! Apply now and start a new chapter as AI/ML Engineer at Dataroots! 🎉

Title: AI ENGINEER / ML ENGINEER
Description:
Beschrijving

In deze functie ben je vanaf het begin betrokken bij AI- en data-gedreven projecten en draag je verantwoordelijkheid voor zowel de analytische als de technische kant. Je analyseert klantvragen en de beschikbare data en vertaalt deze naar realistische en waardevolle oplossingen, nog voordat development start.

Op basis daarvan bepaal je de algemene aanpak en strategie. Je kiest de juiste AI- en machine learning-methoden, brengt risico’s en afhankelijkheden in kaart en ontwerpt de MVP en roadmap. Hoewel je de software niet zelf bouwt, werk je nauw samen met Data Scientists, AI Engineers en Software Engineers om ervoor te zorgen dat de oplossingen haalbaar, schaalbaar en klaar voor productie zijn.

Je werkt met moderne AI-technologieën zoals machine learning, generatieve AI en computer vision. Daarnaast begrijp je hoe datapijplijnen en ETL-processen training en implementatie ondersteunen. Door helder te communiceren stem je klanten en teams goed af op verwachtingen, mogelijkheden en beperkingen, en leg je een sterke basis voor hoogwaardige en toekomstbestendige AI-oplossingen.

Technology stack

Je gaat mogelijk aan de slag met technologieën en/of methodologieën zoals:

Cloud platforms (Azure / AWS)

Natural Language Processing (Text Classification, Clustering algorithms)

Generative AI (LLMs, RAG pipelines, Agentic AI)

Python (FastAPI, NumPy, Pandas, scikit-learn, OpenCV)

Computer Vision (Object Detection, Image Segmentation, Image Classification)

MLOps (Docker, CI/CD)

Onze werkwijze: Agile / Scrum

We zijn op zoek naar een AI Engineer die zich richt op data science en die graag de leiding neemt in complexe AI-projecten en duidelijke richting kan geven.

Title: Data Scientist - Azure/ KNIME/ Python
Description:
NTT DATA is actively looking for a Data scientist for one of its partners in the public sector. This freelance position is based in Brussels. Homeworking is allowed.

The consultant is responsible for designing and setting up a modular and future-oriented machine learning framework.

associated tasks:

Implement best practices for data processing, model training, validation and deployment
Integration of maintenance and feedback mechanisms (monitoring of model performance, retraining)
Manage version management and reproduction of models
Visualize and share results
Equipped with logging and monitoring of the system
Preparing the infrastructure for migration to cloud environments
Documenting processes and working methods
Coaching and supporting team members in using the framework
Presenting analyses and results to business stakeholders
The consultant also develops and applies machine learning models to support business use cases.

Corresponding tasks

Translating business questions into technical solutions in iterative development cycles
Identifying and validating data sources
Conducting data analysis and data preparation
Conduct feature engineering to create relevant variables
Conduct exploratory data analysis (EDA) to evaluate data quality and patterns
Selecting and evaluating suitable algorithms
Developing, comparing and tuning predictive models
Automating the model development process
Visualize and present results in an understandable and convincing manner
Continuous evaluation and optimization of models

Technical skills

Azure ML SDK
Jupyter Notebook
KNIME
Machine learning and AI
Microsoft Azure Machine Learning
Microsoft Fabric
Microsoft SQL database
Oracle Database
Python
SQL

NTT DATA - a part of NTT Group - is a trusted global innovator of IT and business services headquartered in Tokyo. We help clients transform through consulting, industry solutions, business process services, IT modernization and managed services. NTT DATA enables clients, as well as society, to move confidently into the digital future. We are committed to our clients' long-term success and combine global reach with local client attention to serve them in over 50 countries. Visit us at nttdata.com

Title: Decision Intelligence Developer
Description:
Can’t wait to make an impact on the world? You’re not alone. Join us in driving progress in the working world and beyond.

Your journey with us

We’re seeking a talented Data Skill Builder to join our Supply Chain Data Team. In this role, you’ll be the bridge between complex data systems and actionable insights—extracting, transforming, and visualizing data from diverse sources to fuel intelligent decision-making. You’ll work closely with architects and business stakeholders to build robust data pipelines and compelling analytics that drive supply chain optimization. If you’re passionate about turning raw data into business value and thrive in a collaborative, fast-paced environment, this is your opportunity to make a tangible impact.

What You’ll Do


Data Extraction & Integration: Extract data from multiple enterprise systems (ERP, WMS, TMS, planning tools) and third-party sources; design and build reliable data pipelines that consolidate information into unified datasets
Data Analysis & Transformation: Perform exploratory data analysis, identify data quality issues, and transform raw data into clean, structured formats ready for analytics and modeling
Analytics Development: Develop analytical queries, dashboards, and reports that surface key insights; translate business questions into data-driven answers
Data Visualization: Create compelling visualizations and interactive dashboards (Tableau, Power BI) that communicate complex data stories to business stakeholders in clear, actionable ways
Collaboration with Architects: Work closely with Solution Architects to understand technical requirements; implement data solutions according to architectural specifications and best practices
Data Quality & Governance: Implement data validation checks, monitor data quality, and maintain documentation; ensure data consistency and reliability across systems
Continuous Learning: Stay current with data engineering tools, analytics techniques, and emerging technologies; contribute ideas for improving data processes and methodologies


Let's Talk About You


Data Engineering Fundamentals: 3+ years of hands-on experience building data pipelines, ETL processes, and working with SQL and relational/non-relational databases
Programming Proficiency: Strong experience with Python, SQL, or similar languages; ability to write clean, maintainable code and debug technical issues independently
Analytics & Visualization: Demonstrated expertise with analytics tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker) and statistical analysis; ability to translate data into compelling visual narratives
Cloud & Data Platforms: Familiarity with cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GCP) and experience working with data warehouses, data lakes, or big data platforms (Spark, Hadoop)
Problem-Solving Mindset: Strong analytical thinking; ability to break down complex data challenges and find practical solutions with minimal guidance
Communication Skills: Ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders; comfortable presenting findings and collaborating across teams
Passion for Data: Genuine curiosity about data; track record of delivering data projects that drove measurable business insights or operational improvements


Primary Focus: Data Excellence Over Platform Specificity

While Familiarity With Aera Technology Is a Nice-to-have, Data Engineering And Analytics Expertise Is What Matters Most. We Value Professionals Who


Prioritize data quality, pipeline reliability, and analytical rigor above platform-specific knowledge
Can quickly learn new tools and platforms as business needs evolve
Understand when to build custom solutions vs. leverage existing platforms
Bring deep experience with core data technologies: Python, SQL, cloud data warehouses, BI tools, and ML frameworks


Supporting Technologies

Data & Analytics : Python, SQL, Spark, Kafka, TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn, XGBoost

Cloud Platforms: AWS, Azure, GCP

Data Warehousing: Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Delta Lake

Visualization & BI: Tableau, Power BI, Looker

DevOps & Deployment: Git, Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines

Collaboration: Jira, Confluence, design tools

Our story

Enterprise Performance is about achieving maximum impact and value from investments in finance, supply chain, and IT operations requires a holistic view of key business functions from strategy through process design and technology enablement

Deloitte is a globally recognized professional services firm committed to making an impact that matters. Our Supply Chain & Network Operations practice supports clients in addressing their most critical supply chain challenges through strategic, analytical, and technology-enabled projects. We deliver end-to-end, data-driven redesign and digitization to transform key areas such as planning, sourcing & procurement, manufacturing, and logistics & distribution. Join our team in Belgium and work with clients across diverse industries as we advise, implement, and operate solutions that drive sustainable growth and operational excellence.

Who is Deloitte?

We provide industry-leading audit and assurance, tax and legal, consulting and related services. We are committed to driving innovation across offerings to help our clients address their challenges, while giving our professionals opportunities to learn and grow in this era of transformation.

In Belgium, +5000 dedicated professionals active in +10 offices, take great pride in bringing multidisciplinary expertise to a wide variety of clients, from national and international companies, small, fast-growing and large organizations to public institutions and governmental authorities.

Why Deloitte?

Be the true you! We foster diversity and inclusion and encourage you to bring your authentic self to work. Explore, question and collaborate while building a career that inspires and energises you.

Never stop growing! Diversity of thought makes us stronger. At Deloitte, we tailor a personalized learning experience, offering you the opportunity to grow at your own pace and achieve maximum impact.

We practice what we preach! As a Purpose-led organisation, at the heart of everything we do is a set of timeless principles and unifying values .

Life looks different for each of us, so we created a varied benefits package that you can tap into:


My Benefits My Choice, a flexible rewards plan tailored to your lifestyle and priorities
Sustainable transport options offered by Mobility@Deloitte
Flexible work arrangements for all and initiatives supported by Parents & Caregivers @Deloitte
Wellbeing tips and activities powered by Energise@Deloitte
Topped off with other health benefits and insurance opportunities


Empowering our employees with flexible work arrangements remains essential in today's reality:


Hybrid workplace: combination of home office and on-site (+10 offices in Belgium or client's premises).
Part-time employment: all our jobs are open to full-time or part-time work under a 90% or 80% regime.


Join us to make an impact together! Apply now!

Title: Fraud Data Analyst
Description:
EBA CLEARING is a provider of pan-European payment infrastructure solutions. Founded in 1998, the Company is owned by 48 of the major banks operating in Europe and based on a country-neutral governance model. The payment systems of EBA CLEARING are pan-European by design and desire: they are developed in close co-operation with the Company’s multinational user community and best-of-breed technology partners.

Join our team of smart and curious people from all over the world as we help shape the future of pan-European payments.




This position can be located in Brussels or Frankfurt.




The position




The FPAD Competence Centre (FCC) is responsible for the development and maintenance of fraud detection models for EBA CLEARING FPAD functionality. This requires data analysis and modelling as well as a good understanding of fraud developments and the needs of our users, supporting them in understanding FPAD functioning and results and help investigate specific cases.




As a member of the FCC team your responsibilities will include:




monitoring model and data quality in production
finding new emerging fraud characteristics by exploring the vast transaction data underlying our FPAD solution
building new features that help detect fraudulent transactions
developing state-of-the-art AI models against billions of historical transaction
creating documentation and charts to explain your work to our users
sharing insights with experts from financial institutions across Europe
staying up to date with latest trends in fraud detection and drive the development of our solution







The profile




The basics:

University degree in computer science, mathematics or a related field
At least three years of experience working in a quantitative role, in a fraud or risk environment at a bank or payment service provider
Good knowledge of data science and data engineering, preferably with python
Especially experience with
Experience to optimise models on response time
Working in a highly regulated environment
Domain knowledge in payment data
Full proficiency in English, any additional European languages would be an asset




Required skills:

Intercultural communication
Customer-first attitude in everyday responsibilities
Analytical thinking and efficient problem-solving
Ability to anticipate problems, needs and changes and evaluate operational risks
Attention to detail while managing multiple tasks simultaneously
Ability to work autonomously in a fast-paced environment




What we offer

EBA CLEARING is a provider of pan-European payment infrastructure solutions. Founded in 1998, the Company is owned by 48 of the major banks operating in Europe and based on a country-neutral governance model. The payment systems of EBA CLEARING are pan-European by design and desire: they are developed in close co-operation with the Company’s multinational user community and best-of-breed technology partners.

Join our team of smart and curious people from all over the world as we help shape the future of pan-European payments.


This position can be located in Brussels or Frankfurt.


The position


The FPAD Competence Centre (FCC) is responsible for the development and maintenance of fraud detection models for EBA CLEARING FPAD functionality. This requires data analysis and modelling as well as a good understanding of fraud developments and the needs of our users, supporting them in understanding FPAD functioning and results and help investigate specific cases.


As a member of the FCC team your responsibilities will include:


  • monitoring model and data quality in production
  • finding new emerging fraud characteristics by exploring the vast transaction data underlying our FPAD solution
  • building new features that help detect fraudulent transactions
  • developing state-of-the-art AI models against billions of historical transaction
  • creating documentation and charts to explain your work to our users
  • sharing insights with experts from financial institutions across Europe
  • staying up to date with latest trends in fraud detection and drive the development of our solution

The basics:

  • University degree in computer science, mathematics or a related field

  • Experience to optimise models on response time

Title: Senior Data Science Analist
Description:
We're looking for an experienced Machine Learning Consultant to help our client design and deliver a scalable, future-proof machine learning ecosystem. In this role, you will combine technical expertise with business insight, developing robust ML solutions that support strategic decision-making while building a sustainable framework for future AI initiatives. You will work closely with multidisciplinary teams and play an active role in promoting best practices and knowledge sharing.

The Role & Key Responsibilities

As a Machine Learning Consultant, you will:


Design and implement a modular and scalable machine learning framework that supports the full model lifecycle.
Establish best practices for data preprocessing, model training, validation, deployment, and ongoing maintenance.
Integrate monitoring, logging, feedback loops, and model retraining processes to ensure long-term performance and reliability.
Manage model versioning and reproducibility while preparing the technical environment for future cloud migration.
Document methodologies and technical processes, coach team members, and share knowledge across the organisation.
Present technical findings and project outcomes in a clear and accessible way to business stakeholders.



Your Profile

The ideal candidate combines strong machine learning expertise with excellent communication and problem-solving skills. You bring:


Proven experience in designing and implementing end-to-end machine learning solutions.
The ability to translate business challenges into technical solutions using an iterative development approach.
Experience in data sourcing, data preparation, exploratory data analysis (EDA), and feature engineering.
Strong knowledge of algorithm selection, predictive model development, model evaluation, optimisation, and automation.
Experience with model monitoring, performance improvement, and the visualisation of analytical results for both technical and non-technical audiences.
A collaborative mindset with the ability to mentor colleagues and contribute to the growth of a data-driven culture.



What To Expect

Our client offers the opportunity to work on innovative data and AI projects with a direct impact on business operations and strategic decision-making. You will join a collaborative environment where continuous learning and innovation are encouraged. Candidates should be native speakers of one of Belgium's national languages and have a good reading and comprehension level of the other. This position follows a hybrid working model, with two days per week on-site and the remaining days working remotely.

Contactperson & Reference


Reference #: INW26513
Pieter Messely
pieter.messely@i4m.be

Title: AI & Data Engineer
Description:
What You'll Do

Are you the AI & Data Engineer who wants to join our team to develop innovative data pipelines and state-of-the-art AI solutions?

As an AI & Data Engineer at iO, you are first and foremost an experienced software engineer who builds, integrates, and operationalizes AI and data solutions within clients’ existing IT ecosystems.

You will work on a wide variety of AI challenges: from intelligently leveraging or fine-tuning Large Language Models, agent and multi-agent setups, and applying RAG patterns, to building ETL pipelines that feed a data platform. A recurring pattern in your work is integrating existing cloud services and APIs into an efficient, scalable, and reliable solution. The focus lies on building production-grade systems that function correctly within complex, often enterprise-level IT landscapes.

Prompt engineering is an art form to you. You think creatively about how to achieve the best results, while paying attention to preventing hallucinations and implementing smart guardrails. You evaluate your solutions systematically to gain insight into output quality.

In your work, you will encounter many forms of AI: from statistical models to Large Language Models, from Generative AI in the domains of image, video, and audio to Natural Language Processing. The emphasis is mainly on applying and integrating existing models and services, and building the infrastructure around them.

You will work on a wide range of client challenges: from automatically analyzing documents to detecting events in audio or video data, building agents and orchestration solutions, generating graphical output based on data, or creating and validating content. Wherever AI can add value, you design and implement the complete solution as an engineer.

You collaborate with various AI experts within iO and with technical teams on the client side. Your solutions are based on modern (cloud) platforms, SaaS services, and programming languages such as TypeScript and Python.

What Are Your Responsibilities as an AI & Data Engineer?


Designing AI & Data solutions: Analyze client challenges related to AI and data and define solutions, approaches, and architecture
Software engineering & integration: Develop backend components, APIs, and services to ensure AI solutions integrate seamlessly with existing applications and platforms
Data Pipeline Development: Design and build robust ETL/ELT processes and real-time data streams
Data Platform Architecture: Set up modern data warehouses, data lakes, and analytics platforms
AI Integration: Implement machine learning pipelines and integrate AI services into data workflows
Data Quality & Governance: Ensure data quality, security, and compliance within data ecosystems
Performance Optimization: Optimize data processing performance and scalability
Cloud Data Services: Work with cloud-native data services such as AWS Glue, Azure Data Factory, or Google Dataflow
Monitoring & Observability: Implement monitoring and alerting for data pipelines and AI workloads


Your Qualities And Skills As AI & Data Engineer


You hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree with a focus on software engineering
You have several years of experience as a software engineer and have worked on data- and/or AI-driven systems in a professional, preferably enterprise, environment
You have experience with different LLM architectures and understand the strengths and weaknesses of various AI solutions
You are familiar with the principles of modern data architectures, data modeling, and big data processing
You are proficient in modern data processing languages such as Python and SQL, and have experience with frameworks such as Apache Spark, Pandas, or Dask
You are familiar with AWS or Azure cloud data platforms
You have a strong foundation in software engineering, including knowledge of CI/CD, unit testing, Git (flow), secure development, error handling, asynchronous operations, and real-time data streams
You are able to translate your ideas into technical architecture diagrams, data flow schemas, and documentation, while also clearly explaining your solutions to less technical stakeholders (clients and colleagues)
You enjoy sparring with colleagues about ideas and solutions and working collaboratively to achieve better results


What’s in it for you

Vacation icon Vacation icon used in website

32 vacation days

Based on a 40-hour work week, you’ll have 20 vacation days and 12 compensatory days of leave (reduction of working time).

Money icon Money icon used in website

Mobility & end-of-year bonus

After working at iO for 6 months, you can count on an end-of-year bonus. And in terms of mobility, you can choose from various options, depending on your job or preference.

SafeEnvironment icon Safe Environment icon used in website

Hospitalisation insurance

When something unfortunate happens, the last thing you need is the added stress of unexpected costs. That’s why all employees get hospitalisation insurance at Axa.

HybridWorking icon Hybrid Working icon used in website

Hybrid working

A good work-life balance is super important. Together with your team, you’ll decide when and where you work.

FoodBenefits icon Food Benefits icon used in website

Healthy snacks & lunch

At every iO campus, you can grab a healthy snack throughout the day and have a healthy and varied lunch for a small fee.

TrainingBudget icon Training Budget icon used in website

Personal training budget

Each year, you’ll get a budget of €3,000 to develop yourself, in your profession or as a person, through training, events, or courses.

Celebrations icon Celebrations icon used in website

Celebrating together

If there’s something to celebrate, we know how to party! From time to time, the party team at your campus will organise the best (theme) parties.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About the Company







NISH Tech BV is dedicated to supporting the energy transition through innovative data solutions. Our mission is to empower organizations in the energy sector with data-driven insights and tools that facilitate strategic decision-making. We foster a collaborative culture that values expertise, creativity, and a commitment to excellence.







About the Role







NISH Tech is looking for a talented Data Analyst with strong expertise in Python, Power BI, Data Engineering, and Data Visualization to support strategic initiatives within the energy sector. In this role, you will work with large-scale simulation datasets, build analytical tools, create insightful visualizations, and contribute to data-driven decision-making that supports the future of the energy transition. You will collaborate with technical experts, business stakeholders, and management teams to transform complex datasets into meaningful insights and user-friendly visual solutions. This is an excellent opportunity for professionals who enjoy working with large datasets, developing analytical tools, and contributing to innovative projects in the electricity and energy market domain.




Languages: English (mandatory) and Dutch and/or French.







Responsibilities







Data Management & Engineering

Improve and optimize data management processes.
Design and maintain data warehouses, data pipelines, and data integration solutions.
Consolidate historical datasets into centralized repositories.
Develop interfaces and integrations between analytical tools and data platforms.
Ensure data quality, consistency, and reliability across multiple sources.




Data Analysis & Simulation Insights

Analyze large volumes of simulation output data.
Identify trends, patterns, anomalies, and business insights.
Perform comparative analysis between different studies and historical datasets.
Critically evaluate simulation and algorithm outputs and support decision-making processes.




Visualization & Reporting

Create impactful dashboards and visualizations using Power BI.
Benchmark visualizations against leading industry studies and platforms.
Develop dynamic reporting solutions for stakeholders and management.
Design interactive and user-friendly interfaces for data exploration.
Present findings and iterate visual solutions based on stakeholder feedback.




Tool Development

Develop and maintain Python-based analytical tools and scripts.
Automate data processing and reporting workflows.
Create and update scripts to identify inconsistencies in simulation input/output data.
Support continuous improvement of existing analytical and visualization tools.










Qualifications







Must-Have Technical Skills

Minimum 3 years of professional experience as a Data Analyst, Data Engineer, or similar role.
Strong Python development experience.
Advanced Power BI dashboard development and reporting skills.
Experience with data modeling, ETL processes, and data pipelines.
Strong SQL and database knowledge.
Experience working with large and complex datasets.
Data visualization and storytelling expertise.
Understanding of data quality management and validation techniques.




Nice-to-Have Skills

React.js development experience.
Experience with simulation tools, optimization models, or market modeling solutions.
Knowledge of ANTARES or similar simulation platforms.
Experience with data warehouses and cloud-based analytics platforms.
Knowledge of energy market analytics and forecasting models.










Domain Knowledge







Interest in and understanding of the energy sector.
Knowledge of electricity markets, power systems, or energy transition initiatives is highly valued.
Experience working with market simulations, optimization engines, or economic dispatch tools is a strong advantage.







Soft Skills







Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset.
Ability to work independently and proactively.
Excellent stakeholder communication and presentation skills.
Innovative thinker with a continuous improvement mindset.
Collaborative team player capable of working in multidisciplinary environments.
Ability to translate complex technical information into clear business insights.







What We're Looking For










📧 Apply Now: Jobs@nishtech.eu




🌐 Website: https://www.nishtech.be

associated tasks:

Jupyter Notebook
KNIME

Python

We're looking for an experienced Machine Learning Consultant to help our client design and deliver a scalable, future-proof machine learning ecosystem. In this role, you will combine technical expertise with business insight, developing robust ML solutions that support strategic decision-making while building a sustainable framework for future AI initiatives. You will work closely with multidisciplinary teams and play an active role in promoting best practices and knowledge sharing.

The Role & Key Responsibilities

As a Machine Learning Consultant, you will:

  • Design and implement a modular and scalable machine learning framework that supports the full model lifecycle.
  • Establish best practices for data preprocessing, model training, validation, deployment, and ongoing maintenance.
  • Integrate monitoring, logging, feedback loops, and model retraining processes to ensure long-term performance and reliability.
  • Manage model versioning and reproducibility while preparing the technical environment for future cloud migration.
  • Document methodologies and technical processes, coach team members, and share knowledge across the organisation.
  • Present technical findings and project outcomes in a clear and accessible way to business stakeholders.


Your Profile

The ideal candidate combines strong machine learning expertise with excellent communication and problem-solving skills. You bring:

  • Proven experience in designing and implementing end-to-end machine learning solutions.
  • The ability to translate business challenges into technical solutions using an iterative development approach.
  • Experience in data sourcing, data preparation, exploratory data analysis (EDA), and feature engineering.
  • Strong knowledge of algorithm selection, predictive model development, model evaluation, optimisation, and automation.
  • Experience with model monitoring, performance improvement, and the visualisation of analytical results for both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • A collaborative mindset with the ability to mentor colleagues and contribute to the growth of a data-driven culture.


What To Expect

Our client offers the opportunity to work on innovative data and AI projects with a direct impact on business operations and strategic decision-making. You will join a collaborative environment where continuous learning and innovation are encouraged. Candidates should be native speakers of one of Belgium's national languages and have a good reading and comprehension level of the other. This position follows a hybrid working model, with two days per week on-site and the remaining days working remotely.

Title: Data Analyst
HTML:
The consultant will help to perform different tasks within the SMA team:

  • Improve data management process at SMA (set up data warehouse, data pipelines, ..);
  • Explore large amount of data coming out of the simulations;
  • Benchmarking visualizations from other equivalent studies, i.e. ERAA (ERAA 2025 Reports & Results), TYNDP (Explore | ENTSO-E TYNDP, Visualisation Platform | ENTSOs TYNDP 2024 Scenarios), MLZ (Data Publication), NESO-FES (FES 25
  • Interactive dashboard), RTE (Analyses et données), …
  • Benchmarking visualization already available within SMA;
  • Create visuals from given simulation output, including writing and updating appropriate scripts;
  • Present and iterate upon the created visuals with key stakeholders/management to ensure they fulfill their intended purpose
  • Create visuals to compare different studies together or with historical data; - Create/Update scripts to identify inconsistencies in input/output data from the simulations;
  • Propose user-friendly interface to dynamically explore the data, that could be publicly shared;

Example of tasks A non-exhaustive list of tasks (and description) is given below:

  1. Consolidate historical data into a data warehouse;
  2. Create or update tools in Python in order to analyze different output from the ANTARES simulation tool or other solvers;
  3. Create visualizations of the outputs of simulations;
  4. Critically analyze results of algorithms, assist in the identification of key trends
  5. Data engineering and interfaces between tools

Service requirements (hard skills and soft skills):

Hard skills :

  • Python (must have) Python is extensively used in existing tools at SMA. Python is a general-purpose programming language whose design philosophy emphasizes code readability. The outcome of the project must be compatible with the existing toolchain.
  • PowerBI (must have) PowerBI is the central tool used at SMA for visualisation. The project should build on the tools/visuals already available within SMA.
  • React.js (nice to have) React.js is used within SMA for specific types of visualization. Knowledge of React would be a plus
  • ANTARES (mainly the input/output interface but also a general knowledge of economic dispatch tool is a ‘nice to have’) ANTARES is an economic dispatch tool developed by RTE, the French TSO. In the application’s core is an economic dispatch tool for the electricity market. Antares allows to simulate the electricity market on an hourly basis. Any experience with market simulations can be valued here, including other solvers. A minimum knowledge of energy systems is also required.

Soft skills :

  • Medior profile: 3 yrs experience minimum.
  • The consultant has knowledge of and interest in the energy market;
  • Ability to work independently and in a team;
  • The consultant has an analytical and innovative mind;

About the profile:

  • You like diving into data and create clear, insightful, and impactful visuals;
  • Interested to deliver high quality tools to improve the team’s functioning;
  • You are looking to invest a team on the medium term where you could face challenging projects and grow an expertise;
  • You are interested in the energy transition and challenges in the electricity sector.
  • Good level of Dutch and/or French and English is required.

Title: AI ENGINEER
Description:
AI Engineer RAG Python OpenAI

Brussels Permanent position

At eXalt, we build teams, not profiles. As a consulting company, we bring together experts driven by digital transformation, collaboration and real impact.

We are looking for a senior AI Engineer to join us on a permanent contract and design robust, production ready AI solutions. You will work on high impact projects involving RAG, generative AI, intelligent automation and complex system integration, in demanding environments.




Your role

Design and develop AI solutions based on RAG, embeddings, vector databases and LLMs.

Build and industrialize AI and ML pipelines in Python.

Integrate AI APIs such as OpenAI and open source models.

Automate and connect workflows using tools like n8n or UiPath.

Deliver document understanding solutions using OCR, ABBYY, parsing and advanced regex.

Collaborate closely with product and business teams and take part in Agile rituals.

Optimize performance, security and scalability of AI architectures.

Act as a technical reference on AI and automation topics.




Your profile

4 to 8 years of experience in data, AI or intelligent automation.

Strong Python expertise (FastAPI, LangChain, ML frameworks, vector databases).

Solid background in machine learning and generative AI.

Hands on experience with RAG, embeddings, OpenAI API, n8n, UiPath and OCR solutions.

Good understanding of software architectures and process modeling (BPMN, UML).

Comfortable working in Scrum or Agile environments.

Fluent in French (C2) and professional English (C1). Dutch is a plus.




What we value

A strong interest in applied AI and business value.

The ability to explain complex topics clearly.

Autonomy, curiosity and a solution oriented mindset.

A collaborative attitude and the desire to build scalable, useful solutions.




What you will find at eXalt

A permanent position in a fast growing consulting firm.

High impact projects combining AI, automation and innovation.

A strong community of experts across AI, data, development and product.

A human centered environment focused on growth and knowledge sharing.

Title: Data Engineer - QuantumBlack, AI by McKinsey
Description:
Who You'll Work With

Driving lasting impact and building long-term capabilities with our clients is not easy work. You are the kind of person who thrives in a high performance/high reward culture - doing hard things, picking yourself up when you stumble, and having the resilience to try another way forward.

In return for your drive, determination, and curiosity, we'll provide the resources, mentorship, and opportunities you need to become a stronger leader faster than you ever thought possible. Your colleagues—at all levels—will invest deeply in your development, just as much as they invest in delivering exceptional results for clients. Every day, you'll receive apprenticeship, coaching, and exposure that will accelerate your growth in ways you won’t find anywhere else.

When you join us, you will have:


Continuous learning: Our learning and apprenticeship culture, backed by structured programs, is all about helping you grow while creating an environment where feedback is clear, actionable, and focused on your development. The real magic happens when you take the input from others to heart and embrace the fast-paced learning experience, owning your journey.
A voice that matters: From day one, we value your ideas and contributions. You’ll make a tangible impact by offering innovative ideas and practical solutions, all while upholding our unwavering commitment to ethics and integrity. We not only encourage diverse perspectives, but they are critical in driving us toward the best possible outcomes.
Global community: With colleagues across 65+ countries and over 100 different nationalities, our firm’s diversity fuels creativity and helps us come up with the best solutions for our clients. Plus, you’ll have the opportunity to learn from exceptional colleagues with diverse backgrounds and experiences.
World-class benefits: On top of a competitive salary (based on your location, experience, and skills), we provide a comprehensive benefits package to enable holistic well-being for you and your family.


Your Impact

As a Data Engineer I, you will build the foundational data infrastructure that powers cutting-edge AI applications leveraging LLMs, retrieval systems, workflows, and emerging agentic architectures.

You will design and maintain scalable data pipelines, manage secure data environments, and prepare data for AI-driven systems while collaborating with cross-functional teams and clients. You’ll tackle real-world challenges by contributing to the development of next-generation AI systems and grow as a technologist by working alongside diverse experts across industries.

In this role, you will design and build the scalable, reproducible data components essential for machine learning, agentic, and autonomous AI systems. You’ll assess data landscapes, apply data quality fundamentals, and prepare data for AI solutions. Additionally, you’ll learn to translate simple hypotheses into engineered features, manage secure data environments, and contribute to R&D initiatives focused on innovating and scaling next-generation AI capabilities.

Your work will help solve some of the most complex and high-impact challenges facing clients across industries. Collaborating across McKinsey’s QuantumBlack and Labs teams, you’ll help develop innovative AI capabilities and scalable enterprise solutions. You’ll help build robust data foundations for scalable, production-ready AI systems. Your contributions will directly support the firm’s ability to accelerate AI adoption, solve complex business problems at scale, and enable clients to achieve meaningful, lasting impact.

You’ll be based in one of our BeNeLux offices as part of our global Data Engineering community. Working in cross-functional Agile teams, you’ll collaborate closely with Data Scientists, Machine Learning Engineers, and industry experts to deliver AI solutions. By partnering with clients from data owners to C-level executives you’ll help solve complex problems that drive tangible business value and begin to build your skills as a client-facing technologist.

This role offers a unique opportunity to grow at the forefront of AI, data engineering, and emerging agentic technologies. You’ll develop expertise at the intersection of technology and business by tackling diverse challenges in the evolving AI landscape. Working alongside multidisciplinary teams, you’ll gain a holistic understanding of how data engineering enables advanced AI while collaborating with some of the leading AI and data experts in the industry.

Your Qualifications and Skills


Degree in Computer Science/Engineering, or equivalent experience.
0-2+ years’ experience in a data-focused role (internships, academic projects).
Ability to write clean, well-documented code in a modern programming language, with a strong preference for Python and SQL.
Interest in or exposure to data engineering in the context of Agentic AI, Generative AI, Machine Learning, or Business Intelligence across different types of data formats (structured vs unstructured) and processing methods (streaming vs batch).
Familiarity with commonly used data platforms (Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, PSQL, etc.), cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and engineering tools (Pandas, Spark, dbt, LangChain, etc.).
Exposure to modern software development practices, including version control (Git) and foundational concepts in DevOps and MLOps/LLMOps and CI/CD principles.
Strong communication skills, both verbal and written, in English.
Exceptional time management in a complex and largely autonomous work environment.
Willingness to learn quickly and adapt to different project situations and tech stacks.
Experience using coding agents (Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, etc.) is a plus.

Title: BE - Data Engineer
Description:
Overview

Who are we?

CBTW is a global tech solutions company.

We empower businesses with tailored tech and business solutions that drive innovation and sustainable growth.

With over 2 500 experts across 21 countries, we deliver results through flexible engagement models like: whether augmenting teams, managing complex projects, or building long-term operations.

Thanks to our 5 service lines, we specialize in:


AI & Data Platforms,
Cloud & Enterprise Platforms,
Cybersecurity,
Software Engineering,
Strategy, Product & Transformation.


More info on our website: https://cbtw.tech/

What service line will you be part of?

As a Data Engineer, you will join our AI & Data Platforms expertise.

AI & Data Platforms is CBTW's service line dedicated to supporting organizations in leveraging their data and fulfilling their AI ambitions to achieve tangible results. We focus on adoption and measurable impact from day one, combining our comprehensive, end-to-end expertise with our ecosystem of leading partners such as SAS, Snowflake, Microsoft and Databricks.

Our clients rely on us to support with:


Data Platform modernisation, migration and optimisation
AI-driven Business Insights & Customer Analytics
Cutting-edge GenAI products & Agentic AI automation
Unified Data Platform & Cloud Infrastructure
Data & AI Governance
BI & Analytics for business teams


Responsibilities

What is your role?

You will be part of a journey where you will have two impacts: a client impact and an internal impact. For the internal impact, based on your skills and time, you will be joining our internal Data community at CBTW, to share knowledge and best practices. For the client impact, tasks can vary depending on the client and the project, you will mainly:


Design, development and implementation of data pipelines to extract, transform and load data from various sources (ETL processes)
Design and implementation of data models and structures to meet performance, scalability and data integrity requirements
Optimizing database queries to maximize query performance and minimize execution time
Performing data cleansing, transformation and integration to ensure data quality and consistency
Establish monitoring and security mechanisms to ensure data integrity and confidentiality
Work closely with data analysts, data scientists and other stakeholders to understand business requirements and develop appropriate data solutions
Continuously evaluate new technologies and best practices in data engineering to develop innovative solutions


Qualifications

What is your profile?

As a Positive Thinker you have...


A degree in computer science, information systems, engineering or a related discipline
Proven professional experience of at least 3 years in data engineering or in a similar position
Expertise in the development of ETL processes, data modeling and optimization
Very good knowledge of SQL and experience with relational database design
Experience with big data technologies such as Hadoop, Spark or similar frameworks
Good programming skills, preferably in Python or Scala
Familiarity with cloud computing platforms and their services in the area of data analysis and processing is a plus
Fluency in Dutch and/or French and English


What are we offering?

EVOLUTION:


A career development expert will follow up on your professional path within the company and support you in shaping and growing your career,
Catalogue with a wide variety of trainings for hard and soft skills, private language classes, unlimited access to an E-learning platform (Udemy, Code Academy... ) and possibility to get certified every year,
Training days credits.


COLLABORATION:


You will join a community of [job title] experts where colleagues support each other on projects, share knowledge through workshops and events to keep learning and evolving, and build international connections, while also enjoying great moments and drinks together,
Participate in internal projects to innovate and develop our solutions portfolio,
Join one of our collectives (Wellness, Agile, RSE, Gaming ...) and share your interests with other positive thinkers,
You’ll also enjoy several unforgettable company parties throughout the year. Events that CBTW has truly mastered the art of organizing.


ON TOP OF THAT:


We offer a competitive salary with a flexible mobility package that allows you to choose options such as having mobility benefits (electric cars, electric bikes, public transportation subscription).


Your next adventure starts at CBTW!

Do you see a match? We would love to see your application coming in! 😉
